FundingSeries C ($105M, May 2024, led by GIC + Meritech)
Hiringactively hiring
Priority93
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Building 'context store engineered natively for AI' -- vector-native AI search, MCP servers, RAG, LLM eval frameworks; explicit AI Engineering team in New Delhi office.

Outreach Angle

Amit's permission-aware 2-stage Milvus RAG (250+ permissions per query, 500+ concurrent users, 95% MRR) is almost exactly what Atlan's context layer does for enterprise AI agents. Strong match on multi-tenant architecture, embedding model benchmarking, and production-scale retrieval.

Notes

No specific 'AI Engineer' role currently open -- closest is Engineering Manager, Data Platform (India Remote) on Ashby. Active internship program. 201-500 employees so skip founders/CTO in outreach.

Research Summary

Atlan is the 'Context Layer for AI' -- infrastructure that encodes enterprise business context (definitions, policies, lineage) so AI agents reason correctly at inference time. $105M Series C, 400+ enterprise customers (GM, Nasdaq, Workday). Strong India engineering team. No specific AI Engineer role open but EM Data Platform JD shows exactly what they value: multi-tenant retrieval, AI-native metadata, high-scale ingestion, sub-second search. Amit's permission-aware RAG with Milvus 2-index architecture and 250+ permissions per query is a direct technical match. 6 contacts captured: 2 Tier 1 (EM/senior), 2 Tier 3 (IC), 2 Tier 4 (recruiter).

Tailoring Notes

1. Professional Summary rewritten to mirror Atlan's 'context layer' and 'retrieval and context delivery' language. Proof points front-loaded: Milvus 2-index architecture, 250+ permissions per query, 50K+ queries/month, 95% MRR, GPT-4o/Gemini/Qwen3-Coder orchestration. Dropped generic 'AI Engineer' framing in favor of 'context delivery systems'. 2. Technical Skills reordered: Vector DBs and Retrieval moved to first position (core to Atlan's context store product), then LLMs and AI Frameworks (combined LLM + LangChain), then Languages, then Infrastructure. Hardware/IoT bullet dropped entirely -- not relevant to AI platform role. 3. All changes align with JD signals: multi-tenant isolation, sub-second search, high-scale retrieval, AI-native metadata generation, embedding benchmarking.

Contacts & Emails 6 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 6 queued
Viswesh Subramanian Senior Engineering Manager, AI-native context engineering
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: viswesh.subramanian@atlan.com
3 weeks ago ran a free workshop 'From Agent Demo to Agent System' where he argued agent failures are infrastructure problems (not model problems): 'it invents policies that don't exist, forgets customers between sessions, burns money looping.' He covered all nine components of a production agent framework including context engineering and memory. Amit's permission-aware LangGraph pipeline at Vivansh is exactly this -- a context layer that enforces 250+ policies per query before any retrieval happens.
Initial Email
Subject: your agent architecture workshop
Caught your workshop a few weeks ago -- "From Agent Demo to Agent System." The bit about agents inventing policies that don't exist landed close to home. That's exactly what our EHS chatbot was doing before we rebuilt the retrieval layer.

What we ended up with: a 2-stage Milvus pipeline where the first index is just permissions. 250+ access rules resolved before any retrieval happens, cross-referenced with PostgreSQL at query time. LangGraph handles the routing. It's running at 500+ concurrent users and about 1,000 queries/day without hallucinating policy.

I'm two years into production AI -- the 50K queries/month RAG rebuild at Silver Touch (MRR from 45% to 95%), an on-prem OCR pipeline for government docs, and the EHS system above. I've been tracking Atlan since the Activate launch and the Context Agents demo caught my attention (8% to 100% metadata coverage is a real number).

If your team has any interest in someone who has shipped this kind of infrastructure, I'd welcome 15 minutes. If the timing's off, no worries.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1
Subject: re: your agent architecture workshop
Bumping this in case it got buried.

One thing I left out: the embedding benchmarking behind the retrieval layer. Tested BGE-M3, BGE-small/base/large, Stella, and SPLADE on production data before settling on BGE-M3 with a reranker. The latency tradeoff didn't resolve until we layered intent classification on top. Figured that was more relevant given your work on context engineering.

If now's not the right time, totally fine.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 2
Subject: re: your agent architecture workshop
Last note from me. If Atlan isn't actively hiring for AI engineering right now, I get it -- would still appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Either way, good luck with the next WTF is the Context Layer episode.

Birendra Kumar Sahu Distinguished Engineer - AI Expert
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: birendra.sahu@atlan.com
2 weeks ago posted 'Most AI models are built to be capable. The best ones are built to be purposeful.' -- discussing how Anthropic built Claude for reliability over benchmarks. He is also writing 'Agentic AI Systems' book while building third-gen data/AI platform at Atlan. Ex-CTO background means he thinks about architecture at the full-system level.
Initial Email
Subject: on purposeful vs capable -- production angle
Read your post from a couple of weeks ago about Anthropic and purposeful AI. The framing resonated -- "built for benchmark scores vs built for reliability" is the exact tension I've run into rebuilding RAG systems in production.

Quick context on why I'm writing: I've spent the last two years shipping AI that has to work reliably, not just score well. A multi-tenant RAG chatbot at 50K+ queries/month (MRR from 45% to 95% after I rebuilt the retrieval layer), a permission-aware LangGraph pipeline enforcing 250+ access rules per query, a multi-model orchestration pipeline running GPT-4o + Qwen3-Coder + Gemini on one flow. The production-first constraint shapes every architecture decision.

I've been following Atlan since the Activate launch and I'm working through your "Agentic AI Systems" material. The context store problem -- how you deliver trusted enterprise context to agents at inference time -- maps directly to what I've been building, just at the enterprise data layer rather than the EHS domain.

If you'd be open to 15 minutes, I'd like to understand how you think about the retrieval architecture behind the context store. Not asking for secrets, just curious about the tradeoffs you're navigating.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1
Subject: re: on purposeful vs capable -- production angle
Following up in case this got buried.

One more specific detail worth adding: the on-prem OCR pipeline I built at Silver Touch processed 200+ page government PDFs for sensitive docs (Aadhaar, PAN, GST). Used DocTR + DocLink with Llama-3.1-Nemotron-70b, got to about 90% entity extraction accuracy. The constraint was that no data could leave the building. Different flavor of the context delivery problem -- trusted extraction under hard access restrictions.

If now's not the right time, I'm fine circling back.

Mustafa Hasan Khan Senior Software Engineer, AI Observability & LLM-native systems
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: mustafa.khan@atlan.com
IIT Madras grad, 3x GSoC mentor, building AI observability and LLM-native systems at Atlan. Recently shared Atlan's Software Engineer Intern posting and engaged with DuckDB-skills launch. His work on LLM observability pipelines is exactly where Amit's multi-model orchestration experience (GPT-4o + Qwen3-Coder + Gemini on one flow, each stage validated with Pydantic) becomes directly relevant.
Initial Email
Subject: LLM observability + multi-model orchestration
Saw you're building LLM observability pipelines at Atlan. That's the exact layer I've been wishing we had better tooling for at Vivansh.

We run a multi-model flow: GPT-4o for access control and query rewriting, Qwen3-Coder-480B for code-heavy generation, GPT-4o-mini for cheaper classification passes. Each stage is Pydantic-validated with a schema contract. Without proper observability you're flying blind on which model is failing which query type -- we ended up logging at every stage and inspecting by hand, which doesn't scale.

I'm two years into production AI (RAG at 50K queries/month, the multi-model pipeline above, an on-prem OCR system for government docs) and I've been watching Atlan's work since the Activate launch. The Context Agents demo in particular -- the jump from 8% to 100% metadata coverage using AI agents is the kind of production result that takes real infrastructure behind it.

If your team is hiring or planning to hire on the AI side, I'd be curious to hear more about the observability stack you're building. Happy to share notes on the multi-model orchestration setup too.

Amit

amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Job Description
Engineering Manager, Data Platform -- India Remote

Who We Are
Most companies are racing to deploy AI, but very few have the foundation to make it work reliably. Atlan is building that missing layer: the context layer for enterprise AI. We connect the business context behind data so humans and agents can operate with far more accuracy and confidence.

With backing from world-class investors including GIC, Insight Partners, Meritech, Peak XV, and Salesforce Ventures, we've earned the trust of most AI-forward enterprises like General Motors, Nasdaq, Workday and Elastic.

The Opportunity
Atlan is building the context layer for the AI era, the control plane that makes enterprise data usable, governed, extensible, and AI-ready.

We're looking for an Engineering Manager to join one of our core platform teams. This is not a coordination role. We need an engineer-first, hands-on platform leader who has built and owned foundational systems at scale, whether that's distributed platform primitives and ingestion infrastructure, or data quality systems and AI-native product surfaces that sit on top of them.

Depending on the team you join, you'll own one of two high-leverage problem spaces:

Data Quality & Context Intelligence - proactive detection and resolution of data issues (schema drift, freshness, anomalies) at scale; and using AI to auto-generate metadata context (descriptions, classifications, lineage) for thousands of undocumented enterprise data assets.

App Platform - the shared runtime, SDK, lifecycle, and distribution infrastructure that powers every app, agent, and workflow built on Atlan's platform.

What You'll Do

Build the AI-ready context foundation
Lead the engineering charter to design and evolve an AI-ready context store across structured, unstructured, vector, and graph systems. This platform powers apps, agents, and enterprise workflows on top of trusted data.

Own core platform primitives
Drive end-to-end delivery of complex platform capabilities, including:
- High-scale ingestion and validation frameworks
- Contract-driven routing, storage, and serving layers
- Multi-tenant isolation and noisy-neighbor resilience
- Search APIs with sub-second P95 response times
- Systems operating at 99.99% availability

Lead from the front
Engage deeply in architecture and systems design. Guide distributed systems tradeoffs, review RFCs, unblock critical issues, and participate meaningfully in technical decisions. This role requires technical depth, not just coordination.

Scale teams and operational excellence
Build and mentor high-performing engineers and managers. Define engineering metrics, on-call practices, SLO discipline, and incident processes. Strengthen the operational engine behind a platform serving 100K+ users and billions of assets.

What Great Looks Like

Experience building data or platform primitives at scale:
- Data ingestion frameworks or lakehouse foundations
- Metadata, governance, or catalog platforms
- Multi-tenant SaaS infrastructure and tenant isolation mechanisms
- Search, storage, or distributed serving layers
- Data quality, validation, or contract enforcement systems

Platform leadership experience: led and scaled engineering teams, ideally within platform, infrastructure, or data-heavy SaaS organizations.

Strong distributed systems and reliability depth: credibility in distributed systems, cloud-native architectures, and operating high-availability platforms with strict SLOs.

Builder mindset with high agency: thrive in ambiguity, move fast with ownership, build foundational systems from first principles.

AI-native curiosity: actively explore AI-assisted development workflows and think critically about how agentic systems will reshape developer productivity and platform design.

Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

$25M-$29M Series A led by Lightspeed (2025); building skill/tool layer for AI agents — core fit for Amit's LLM orchestration + Python + production AI; agents interact with 150+ apps using GPT-4o/Gemini/Llama.

Notes

YC + a16z-adjacent; ~59 employees, hiring fast. Founded 2023 by IIT alums. Cold email: emphasize agent tool-calling, MCP, GPT-4o/Gemini production.

Research Summary

Composio ($25M Series A, Lightspeed) builds the skills/integration layer for AI agents -- 1000+ app connectors, MCP Gateway, Agent Orchestrator. 59 employees, 3x ARR growth in 2026, 17 open roles. The Bangalore role is MTS - Enterprise Platform: build governance, observability, and control planes for MCP-powered agent ecosystems plus multi-tenant integration gateway. Amit's permission-aware RAG (250+ perms/query, 500+ concurrent users), LangGraph agentic orchestration over 10+ APIs, and production Python/FastAPI stack maps directly. 7 contacts: 1 Tier 1 (AI/Agents Lead), 2 Tier 2 (founders), 2 Tier 3 (ICs), 2 Tier 4 (recruiters).

Tailoring Notes

1. Professional Summary rewritten to lead with 'permission-aware LangGraph agent orchestration enforcing 250+ access controls per query across 500+ concurrent users' and 'tool-calling over 10+ platform APIs' -- directly mirrors JD language around agent infra, permissioning, and platform scale. Added 'multi-tenant AI platforms' to match the enterprise multi-tenancy focus. 2. Technical Skills reordered: Agent and LLM Stack moved to second line (after Languages) to surface MCP, LangGraph, tool-calling agents immediately -- JD's core requirements. Added 'MCP', 'Anthropic Claude', 'Gemini 2.5 Pro', 'Qwen3-Coder' to LLM Stack. Hardware/IoT skills line removed entirely (not relevant to enterprise platform role). 3. Projects section: replaced Voicer + WeepScope (hardware TinyML) with Air Writing Recognition System (IIT Delhi research) -- keeps a strong research credential without hardware/IoT distraction. 4. Certifications: replaced Smart India Hackathon with Samsung Solve for Tomorrow (Top 10 among 70,000+, CNN News18) -- stronger signal, national scale, media validation.

Contacts & Emails 7 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 7 queued
Venkateswara Rao Ch AI/Agents Lead at Composio
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: venkat@composio.dev
AI/Agents Lead at Composio; ex Director of Engineering at 0xPPL; 2x ICPC World Finalist; IIT Bombay CSE. Leads the engineering team building the agent/tool infrastructure layer in Bengaluru.
Initial Email
Subject: LangGraph agent over 10+ APIs -- question about Composio's orchestration approach
Hi Venkat,

Came across Composio's Agent Orchestrator last week -- the approach of managing 20+ parallel coding agents with a self-building architecture is something I have been thinking about from a different angle.

Background: I spent the last year building a LangGraph-orchestrated agent for a multi-tenant EHS platform. Two Milvus indexes (permission + content), tool-calling over 10+ platform APIs, 250+ access controls enforced per query, 500+ concurrent users. The hardest part was getting the permission-filtering and tool-routing to compose cleanly as the graph grew -- which is the same class of problem Composio is solving at the integration layer.

I saw the MTS Enterprise Platform role in Bengaluru. If your team is looking for someone who has shipped this kind of agent infra in production rather than demos, I think the overlap is real.

Happy to share more detail on the 2-stage retrieval setup if useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1
Subject: re: LangGraph agent over 10+ APIs -- question about Composio's orchestration approach
Hi Venkat,

Following up on this. Saw the Agent Orchestrator hit 4,200+ GitHub stars since I wrote -- that growth rate says something about the timing.

One thing I am curious about: the MTS role mentions 'platform primitives for scale' and getting multi-tenancy right without linear engineering cost. That was the exact problem I ran into at Vivansh -- the permission index had to stay separate from the content index precisely because permissions change frequently and you can't re-index the whole corpus on every role update.

Worth a call to compare notes on the architecture?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 2
Subject: re: LangGraph agent over 10+ APIs -- question about Composio's orchestration approach
Hi Venkat,

Last note from me. If the timing is not right or the role is filled, totally fine.

Either way, rooting for what Composio is building with the MCP Gateway. The enterprise permission layer is the hardest part of this space.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Soham Ganatra Founder @ Composio
Founder queued LinkedIn
Email: soham@composio.dev
2 days ago posted about hosting a private AI agent builder dinner in SF. Reposted Composio's 'Similarity is not relevance' blog post (RAG retrieval quality critique) -- directly about the production RAG accuracy problem Amit has been solving.
Initial Email
Subject: the 'similarity is not relevance' piece -- ran into this at 50K queries/month
Hi Soham,

Your repost of the 'Similarity is not relevance' piece caught my attention. That exact gap between similarity scores and retrieval quality was what drove a year of work at Silver Touch -- we were at 45% MRR on FAISS before I tested Milvus on production data and got to 95%. The culprit in our case was embedding model mismatch plus missing reranking, not the vector DB itself. BGE-M3 plus a reranker got us there, but the diagnosis took months of benchmarking to isolate.

At my current role at Vivansh Infotech, the retrieval problem got harder: 250+ user permissions that vary per query, 500+ concurrent users, no viable caching because every user's answer is different. Had to build a 2-stage setup with a dedicated permission index in Milvus before the content retrieval even runs.

That class of problem -- permission-aware, multi-tenant agent retrieval -- is exactly what Composio's enterprise platform needs to get right. Saw the MTS Enterprise Platform role in Bengaluru. Happy to talk about whether my background is a fit.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1
Subject: re: the 'similarity is not relevance' piece -- ran into this at 50K queries/month
Hi Soham,

Following up. The MCP Gateway content on SOC2 and RBAC that Composio has been publishing is interesting -- the auth and permission routing layer is where enterprise deals get stuck.

That was the hard part on our side too. At Vivansh, the permission-filtering step runs before retrieval, not after. Doing it post-retrieval at 500+ concurrent users would have been a latency disaster.

If you are looking for someone who has shipped multi-tenant agent infra in production, I would appreciate 15 minutes.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

Subject: re: the 'similarity is not relevance' piece -- ran into this at 50K queries/month

Job Description
Member of Technical Staff - Enterprise Platform
Location: Bangalore
Employment Type: Full time
Location Type: On-site
Department: Engineering

About Composio
We're building the learning infrastructure that transforms AI agents into true digital workers. While today's agents can reason and plan, they fail to do meaningful work because they lack real experience operating in apps. Composio gives agents continuously improving, reusable skills across 1000+ production-grade app connectors including Gmail, Linear, and Hubspot. We handle authentication, tool routing, retries, failure handling, and observability, making every action safe and dependable.

With a $25M Series A led by Lightspeed and 3x ARR growth since the beginning of 2026, we're scaling fast and building for durability.

About the Role
Every enterprise is racing to make AI work -- not as a demo, but as infrastructure that runs their business. Composio is becoming the critical layer that makes this possible: the platform that connects AI agents to 250+ real-world applications with production-grade auth, execution, and reliability.

We've built this for the cloud. Now we need to build it for the enterprise -- and that means rethinking the platform from the ground up with the right abstractions, primitives, and architectural decisions that let us serve a massive, diverse set of enterprise customers without bespoke engineering for each one. This is a founding role. You'll define how Composio shows up in the enterprise world.

Your Impact

Agent infrastructure platform: The foundational layer that enterprise AI agents run on -- governance, observability, and control planes for MCP-powered agent ecosystems. You'll define how organizations monitor, audit, and manage AI agents operating at scale across their systems

The integration gateway: The secure, reliable bridge between an enterprise's AI agents and the outside world -- every SaaS tool, internal system, and API they need to act on. Not just connectors, but a platform-grade gateway with the right trust, permissioning, and routing primitives

Platform primitives for scale: Multi-tenancy, isolation, configuration, and extensibility abstractions that let Composio serve thousands of enterprise customers without linear engineering cost

Enterprise-grade architecture: Deployment flexibility, security, and compliance as first-class platform capabilities -- not bolted-on afterthoughts

The repeatable deployment motion: Turn enterprise onboarding from a services engagement into a product experience. Shorter cycles, fewer custom touches, more self-serve

What you bring

You've built platforms at genuine scale -- not just high user counts, but high complexity: many customer types, deployment models, and integration surfaces

You think in abstractions and primitives. Your instinct is to find the right foundational model, not to solve each problem individually

You've shipped enterprise product capabilities (deployment flexibility, security, admin tooling, compliance) and understand them as product problems, not just checkboxes

You've built or shipped an AI product -- or you're the person who can't stop tinkering. You're building agents on weekends, stress-testing the latest models, experimenting with MCP, and forming your own opinions on where agent architectures are headed. You have a point of view on this space, not just a resume line

You're a force multiplier. When you join a team, the entire product moves faster because the platform decisions are right

This role demands deep platform thinking. You've designed systems where the abstractions were the product -- where getting the primitives right meant the difference between a product that scales and one that drowns in customer-specific code.

You've done this inside large organizations and seen what enterprise-grade actually means when thousands of teams depend on your platform. But you've also operated in environments where you had to build fast, make tradeoffs, and ship before the architecture was perfect.

The combination matters. Big-company pattern recognition with small-company intensity.

What We Offer

Lunch and dinner provided in the office
$200/month learning and development budget
$1,000/month AI tool experimentation budget to automate, accelerate, and improve how you work
High-ownership role with direct exposure to leadership and company-building decisions
Competitive salary and equity

Coreworks AI

Bengaluru · Senior AI Engineer / Founding Engineer

Flipkart

Bengaluru · SDE II / SDE III - GenAI / ML Engineer
done
LocationBengaluru
RoleSDE II / SDE III - GenAI / ML Engineer
Salary22-38 LPA
Hiringactively hiring
Priority98
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Flagship GenAI assistant Flippi (LLM+RAG+NER+Query Reformulation, arXiv 2507.05788); explicitly looks for Python + RAG + prompt engineering at SDE II level (1+ yr Python ML libs).

Notes

Top ML team in Indian e-commerce; Mayur Datar leads. Multimodal search, seller GenAI, Triksha security. Strong RAG/LLM cold-email fit.

Research Summary

Flipkart's GenAI team published the Flippi paper (arXiv 2507.05788) covering LLM+RAG+NER+query reformulation for shopping -- strong technical match with Amit's retrieval work. SDE II Gen AI role sits in Consumer Shopping Experiences, requiring Python, RAG, prompt engineering, LLM APIs. Email pattern is firstname.lastname@flipkart.com. Targeting 3 tech recruiters since cold outreach to EMs at this scale rarely lands; TA team was recently recognized as AI Pioneer at LinkedIn Talent Awards.

Tailoring Notes

1. SUMMARY: Changed 'GenAI' to 'Generative AI (GenAI)' and 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation' spelled out -- ATS exact-string matching. Removed 'edge ML' mention (irrelevant to LLM-infra role). Added 'prompt engineering' and 'vector databases' as explicit keywords mirroring JD requirements. 2. SECTION ORDER: Moved Technical Skills BEFORE Work Experience -- ATS systems front-load keyword matching on the skills section; this ensures RAG, Prompt Engineering, NLP, Python register early in the parse. 3. SKILLS REORDER: 'Techniques' (RAG, Prompt Engineering, NLP, Semantic Search) moved to second bullet, immediately after Python. Added 'Semantic Search' and 'Text Summarization' (both in JD nice-to-haves). 'Infrastructure and Cloud' renamed and added GCP + Microsoft Azure explicitly. Removed Hardware/IoT bullet entirely. Added LangGraph to AI/ML Frameworks. 4. VIVANSH BULLETS (5 -> 4): Bullet 1: added 'conversational assistant' (JD mentions conversational assistants). Bullet 2: added 'prompt engineering for query rewriting' so keyword appears in experience context. Bullet 3: merged form-builder outcome + Pydantic/multi-model detail; added 'Generative AI pipeline' phrase. Bullet 4: added 'semantic search' to video retrieval description. 5. SILVER TOUCH: Added 'Generative AI' to bullet 1. Added 'semantic search' to bullet 2. Added 'NLP-based' to latency bullet. Shortened OCR bullet label (removed full OCR acronym expansion). 6. IIT DELHI: Trimmed to 2 bullets; added 'deep learning model' phrasing for keyword clarity. 7. CERTIFICATIONS: Added Samsung Top 10 achievement (previously only in Projects section which was removed). Added 'Generative AI, NLP, and knowledge mining' to Azure cert description to mirror JD language. 8. ATS RATIONALE: JD keywords now appear at least twice each -- RAG (summary + skills + 3 bullets), Prompt Engineering (summary + skills + bullet), Python (skills), Generative AI (summary + skills + 2 bullets), NLP (skills + bullet), AWS/Azure (summary + skills + bullets), Semantic Search (skills + bullet), Conversational (chatbot bullet).

Contacts & Emails 3 contacts · 3 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 0 queued
Deepa Rani Senior Recruiter Tech @ Flipkart
Recruiter follow_up_1_sent LinkedIn
Email: deepa.rani@flipkart.com
Focuses specifically on tech hiring at Flipkart; part of the TA team named AI Pioneer at LinkedIn Talent Awards 2026. Flippi RAG paper (arXiv 2507.05788) directly relevant to Amit's retrieval work.
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, Azure AI cert
Hi Deepa,

Noticed your team was named AI Pioneer finalist at the LinkedIn Talent Awards. Good recognition for the group.

Reaching out about the SDE II Gen AI opening. Quick background: 2+ years in production AI at Vivansh Infotech. RAG chatbots at 95% MRR and 50K queries per month, on-prem OCR pipelines for sensitive docs, multi-model orchestration across GPT-4o, Qwen, and Gemini. Azure AI Engineer Associate certified. Research stint at IIT Delhi before that.

Read the Flippi arXiv paper (2507.05788). The 2-stage RAG with NER and query reformulation is close to retrieval work I did from scratch at Vivansh. The e-commerce context specifically is what drew me to this role.

Resume and projects: amitgohel.dev

If the role is still open and this sounds like a fit, happy to do a quick screen whenever works.

Amit
Follow-up 1 sent 2026-05-14
Subject: re: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, Azure AI cert
Hi Deepa,

One thing I should have added: last week I ran a comparison of BGE-M3 vs. text-embedding-3-large on an e-commerce product query set. The recall gap at k=10 was bigger than most public benchmarks suggest. Happy to share the numbers if that adds useful context for evaluating fit.

If timing isn't right, no problem.

Amit
Follow-up 2
Subject: re: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, Azure AI cert
Hi Deepa,

Last note from me. If the Gen AI headcount is full or timing is off, totally understood. Would just appreciate being on your radar for future openings.

Amit

Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, open to SDE II Gen AI
Hi Anamika,

Saw you've been posting about SDE2 and SDE3 openings at Flipkart. The Gen AI role looks like a direct match, so reaching out.

Two years shipping production AI at Vivansh Infotech: RAG chatbots at 95% MRR and 50K queries per month, on-prem OCR for sensitive documents, multi-model pipelines with GPT-4o, Qwen, and Gemini. Azure AI Engineer Associate certified. Research at IIT Delhi before that.

The RAG and prompt engineering focus in the JD is the core of what I've been building, not adjacent to it.

Resume and projects: amitgohel.dev

Should I apply through the portal, or is there a better first step?

Amit
Follow-up 1 sent 2026-05-14
Subject: re: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, open to SDE II Gen AI
Hi Anamika,

Quick addition to my last note: I also built multi-tenant permission-aware RAG, where the retrieval index is scoped per seller or user group. In an e-commerce context with seller-level data isolation, that architecture difference matters more than most generic RAG benchmarks show.

Happy to talk through it if useful.

Amit
Follow-up 2
Subject: re: AI/ML engineer, 2 yrs production RAG, open to SDE II Gen AI
Hi Anamika,

Last one from me. If the role is filled or timing is off, no worries. Would be glad to stay on your radar for future Gen AI openings at Flipkart.

Amit

Job Description
Software Development Engineer II, Gen AI -- Flipkart (Consumer Shopping Experiences, Bengaluru)

Responsibilities:
- Work closely with leadership and stakeholders to identify AI opportunities and develop strategies for integrating GenAI technologies into Flipkart's business operations
- Collaborate with cross-organizational teams to integrate GenAI-driven solutions into existing applications
- Implement Gen AI paradigms such as prompt engineering and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
- Deliver solutions following modern software development practices, including Agile methodologies, test-driven development (TDD), and scalable architecture design
- Mentor and guide team members in understanding and applying large language models effectively, fostering a culture of innovation and continuous learning

Requirements:
- Bachelor's degree in computer science, IT, AI, or a relevant discipline
- Proficiency (1+ years) in Python and related AI/ML libraries
- Good understanding of Gen AI paradigms such as Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture and prompt engineering techniques
- Experience with API-based integrations and cloud deployment (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP)

Nice to have:
- Hands-on experience with Generative AI tech: OpenAI / Gemini models or other open source LLMs and large vision/multimedia models
- Experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or equivalent
- Experience in one or more of the following domains: search, recommender systems, NLP/text summarization, computer vision / visual search, conversational assistants

Fractal Analytics

Bengaluru · Senior Data Scientist - Generative AI / AI Engineer - LLMOps (2-5 yrs)
done
LocationBengaluru
RoleSenior Data Scientist - Generative AI / AI Engineer - LLMOps (2-5 yrs)
Salary18-32 LPA
Hiringactively hiring
Priority96
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Fortune 500 AI partner with public GenAI/LLM offerings (LLM Studio on NVIDIA, Kyron AI); active GenAI Data Scientist + AI Engineer LLMOps roles in Bengaluru; Python/PyTorch/LLM/RAG/AWS stack.

Notes

57+ India roles on Workday; ~66 open per Glassdoor; multiple Bengaluru GenAI/LLM postings.

Research Summary

Fractal Analytics is a publicly listed (NSE) Fortune 500 AI partner with 5K-10K employees building enterprise GenAI and agentic AI products. The Bengaluru team is actively hiring Data Scientists for GenAI and Agentic Systems (SR-42113, posted 8 days ago). Key recent signals: LLM Studio at NVIDIA GTC March 2026, Cogentiq agentic platform July 2025, AWS/Microsoft AI partnerships. The stack (Python, LangChain, RAG, vector DBs, fine-tuning, FastAPI) maps exactly to Amit's production work. 6 contacts: 2 Tier 1 (AI Growth Lead, Lead Data Scientist), 2 Tier 3 (Senior Data Scientists), 2 Tier 4 (TA Managers for analytics hiring).

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to mirror JD language: 'Python-based enterprise AI', 'agentic LangChain pipelines', 'multi-model LLM orchestration across GPT-4o, Anthropic Claude, and Gemini 2.5 Pro' (JD lists all three providers explicitly), '111% MRR improvement from FAISS' (JD asks for RAG with dense/sparse retrieval and benchmarking). Technical Skills reordered: Vector Databases promoted to #2 (JD prominently lists ChromaDB, PGVector, Elasticsearch, Milvus); LangChain/LangGraph moved to front of AI/ML Frameworks (JD emphasizes agentic frameworks); Anthropic Claude added explicitly to LLMs list; COT/ReAct added to Techniques prompt engineering detail (JD specifically calls these out); AWS Bedrock and Azure/GCP added to Infrastructure (JD requires cloud platform knowledge); Hardware/IoT bullet dropped (pure software DS role). Projects section dropped -- Voicer + WeepScope are TinyML/hardware, no relevance to GenAI/Agentic DS role. Samsung Solve for Tomorrow moved to Certifications to preserve national-recognition signal without hardware project framing. IIT Delhi trimmed from 3 to 2 bullets: dropped the hardware wearable detail (ESP32/IMU), kept 1,000+ model configs evaluation depth (signals benchmarking rigor that matches JD's evaluation metrics requirement) and IISc/Howard collaboration (pedigree signal relevant for Fractal's Fortune 500 academic-partnership culture). Vivansh video course pipeline bullet dropped; replaced video bullet with Pydantic multi-model pipeline bullet reworded to call out Anthropic Claude and Gemini variants alongside GPT-4o (mirrors JD LLM list).

Contacts & Emails 6 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 6 queued
Raghav Ramamoorthy AI Growth Lead at Fractal
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: raghav.ramamoorthy@fractal.ai
Describes his work as helping Fortune 500s implement Agentic AI and Gen AI at scale. Active in Fractal's LLM Studio rollout at NVIDIA GTC 2026 and Cogentiq enterprise deployments -- exactly the production-scale enterprise AI problem Amit has been solving.
Initial Email
Subject: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
Hi Raghav,

Went through the LLM Studio coverage from NVIDIA GTC last month. The AutoLLM plus LLMOps split addresses something I've run into building production RAG: the gap between a benchmark-passing system and one that holds at enterprise query volume.

I've spent two years on that gap. At Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing six embedding models on production data. At Vivansh I built the same kind of system at 500+ concurrent users with 2-stage retrieval and 250+ per-query access control checks.

The Fortune 500 AI rollouts you work on are exactly where retrieval architecture choices compound. If the Bengaluru team is hiring on the AI engineering side, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how you think about the accuracy problem at that scale. Tuesday or Thursday next week works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-23
Subject: re: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
Hi Raghav,

Following up in case this got buried. One thing I left out: the multi-tenant access control layer at Vivansh made the problem harder than standard RAG. Permission filtering and retrieval accuracy pull against each other, and a 2-index architecture was the only solution that held at 500+ concurrent users.

If timing is not right, no problem.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 2 sched 2026-05-30
Subject: re: production RAG at Fortune 500 scale
Hi Raghav,

Last note from me. If Fractal's Bengaluru team is not actively looking right now, totally understood. Would appreciate being on your radar when it comes up.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Aiswarya Ramachandran Lead Data Scientist at Fractal
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: aiswarya.ramachandran@fractal.ai
Posted about RAG production tradeoffs: retrieval quality vs latency is the core challenge; reranking + larger candidate sets + richer context each add latency. Also posted about fine-tuning with LoRA adapters (50K curated reviews, HuggingFace PEFT on Azure GPU) to handle domain-specific vocabulary gaps in smaller models -- identical problems to what Amit solved at Silver Touch and Vivansh.
Initial Email
Subject: your RAG latency post last month
Hi Aiswarya,

Read your post about production RAG tradeoffs. The tension between retrieval quality and latency (reranking improves accuracy but each added step costs latency) is exactly the problem I spent a year working through at Silver Touch.

My solution there: tested six embedding models on production data before landing on BGE-M3 plus a reranker, then layered intent classification on top to bring latency back down. At Vivansh I built 2-stage retrieval across 10,000+ chunks at 500+ concurrent users, with GPT-4o handling query rewriting. Your point about domain vocabulary gaps in smaller models also tracked with what I saw in production. A PEFT-fine-tuned model is often the right call when a frontier model is too expensive per query.

I've been tracking what Fractal's Bengaluru team is building. If there's a fit on the AI engineering side, I'd appreciate 15 minutes. Tuesday or Thursday works.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

Subject: re: your RAG latency post last month

Following up in case this got lost. One detail I left out: at Silver Touch the FAISS to Milvus migration was the easy part. Getting the embedding evaluation to reflect production query distributions rather than benchmark scores was harder, and that is where the six-model test on real data mattered.

If timing is not right, no worries.

Subject: agentic AI reliability and test control

Your focus on agentic frameworks and test and control stood out while I was looking into Fractal's Bengaluru engineering team.

At Vivansh I built a multi-model orchestration pipeline and found that a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema was what made it production-stable. GPT-4o and Claude both failed to reliably generate the platform's native JSON format without that validation layer. That reliability design sits at the intersection of agentic pipeline architecture and production testing, which is exactly what your tagline points at.

I've been tracking what Fractal is building with Cogentiq. If the Bengaluru team is hiring, would you know the right person to connect with?

Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-26
Subject: re: agentic AI reliability and test control
Hi Kratika,

Following up in case this got lost. The piece I think is underrated in agentic systems: most failures are not model failures. They are schema or state propagation failures between agent steps. That is the problem the Pydantic layer solved in production, and why I think test and control deserves its own architectural layer.

Amit

Job Description
Data Scientist / Sr. Data Scientist - GenAI & Agentic Systems

About Fractal
Fractal is one of the most prominent players in the Artificial Intelligence space. Fractal's mission is to power every human decision in the enterprise. Fractal provides Al to Fortune 500 companies such as Pfizer, Unilever, Walmart, Microsoft, General Mills, etc. to transform their business and power their decisions.

Role Overview
As a Data Scientist / Sr. Data Scientist with a focus on Generative AI and Agentic Systems, you will design and build advanced AI solutions using LLMs, RAG pipelines, and multi-agent frameworks for enterprise clients.

Key Responsibilities
- Architect and deploy cutting-edge LLM-based solutions and agentic workflows
- Build RAG pipelines with knowledge retrieval (dense/sparse retrieval techniques)
- Own full lifecycle: problem scoping, model design, deployment, post-production optimization
- Develop reusable frameworks, pipelines, and prompt libraries for GenAI
- Work with cross-functional teams to integrate solutions into core client systems
- Apply strong analytical and problem-solving skills to enterprise data challenges

Required Skills
- ML/Deep Learning fundamentals (Transformers, LSTMs, RNNs, supervised/unsupervised)
- GenAI expertise: GPT-4, Claude, Llama; fine-tuning and optimization (PEFT, RLHF)
- NLP: text preprocessing, embeddings, sentiment analysis, classification, summarization, Q&A, Document AI
- RAG pipelines with knowledge retrieval (dense/sparse)
- Python, PyTorch, Hugging Face libraries
- LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases (ChromaDB, PGVector, Elasticsearch, Milvus)
- Azure OpenAI, Google Vertex AI, AWS Bedrock
- Advanced prompt engineering: COT, TOT, ReAct, agents
- Cloud platforms: Azure, AWS, GCP (certification preferred)
- Application development: Python, Docker, FastAPI/Django/Flask, Git
- Evaluation metrics and benchmarking for generative models

Location: Bengaluru, India
Seniority: 2-5 years experience

Hasura (PromptQL)

Bengaluru · Software Engineer - PromptQL AI
done
LocationBengaluru
RoleSoftware Engineer - PromptQL AI
Salary28-40 LPA
Hiringactively hiring
Priority97
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

PromptQL is Hasura's flagship enterprise GenAI/RAG platform on Hasura DDN; team hires across LLM agents, retrieval, runtime infra — Amit's Milvus/Qdrant/RAG/Python is exact match.

Notes

PromptQL is Hasura's strategic 2025-26 bet. Bengaluru + SF. Watch Freshteam closely for mid-level backfills.

Research Summary

PromptQL (formerly Hasura) is a $1B+ enterprise AI company building accurate, steer-able AI on top of enterprise data via Hasura DDN. The engineering team is split Bengaluru/SF. The open role targets Python engineers who can work across AI assistant, data engine, and runtime infra layers. Recent signals: Forbes 'MIT Says 95% Enterprise AI Fail' coverage, $900/hr consulting practice launch, Berkeley EPIC Lab DAB benchmark collaboration, and PromptQL Program API (April 2026). Amit's RAG/Milvus/LLM work is a direct technical match. 7 contacts captured: 2 Tier 1 (Director of Eng, Staff Engineer), 3 Tier 3 ICs, 2 Tier 4 HR.

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to mirror JD language: 'Python-based AI systems on enterprise data', 'retrieval, inference, and runtime infrastructure layers', and explicit mention of Anthropic (PromptQL uses Claude) alongside OpenAI and Gemini -- JD says LLM experience is a big plus and lists Anthropic as a provider. Technical Skills reordered: LLM Providers and APIs promoted to #2 slot (Python stays #1) with Anthropic Claude listed prominently; AI/ML Frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph, VLLM) moved to #3 to mirror JD emphasis on LLM infrastructure; TinyML dropped from Techniques (pure software role); Selenium and FFmpeg merged into Infrastructure bullet instead of separate Tools line; Hardware/IoT stays last. Projects section dropped entirely (Voicer + WeepScope are TinyML/hardware -- JD says 'build across AI assistant, data engine, and runtime infrastructure', no hardware relevance). IIT Delhi trimmed from 3 to 2 bullets: dropped the collaboration bullet (IISc/Howard University), kept the ML evaluation depth (1,000+ model configs) which signals data-driven decision-making relevant to PromptQL's benchmarking culture. Samsung Solve for Tomorrow achievement moved into Certifications section to preserve the national-recognition signal without the hardware project framing.

Contacts & Emails 7 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 7 queued
Manushi Khanna Director of Engineering
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: manushi.khanna@promptql.io
Posted 1 month ago about PromptQL shifting teams from Slack conversations to autonomous execution -- 'work is already in motion' -- and reposted the Berkeley EPIC Data Lab DAB benchmark collaboration showing most LLMs fail on enterprise data
Initial Email
Subject: saw your PromptQL execution post last month
Hi Manushi,

Your LinkedIn post last month about PromptQL shifting from tagging people to get something moving to threads where work is already in motion caught my attention. You reposted the Berkeley EPIC Lab DAB benchmark the same day. The accuracy problem in enterprise AI lives in context and retrieval architecture, not in the models themselves.

I've spent two years on that exact problem. At Vivansh I built a permission-aware RAG chatbot on Milvus with 2-stage retrieval: 250+ per-query access control checks, 10K+ content chunks, 80% MRR with GPT-4o. At Silver Touch I rebuilt production RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing 6 embedding models on production-scale data.

If PromptQL's Bengaluru team is hiring, I'd appreciate 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the retrieval layer. Tuesday or Thursday next week works.

Amit

Initial Email
Subject: infra angle on PromptQL's retrieval pipeline
Hi Karthik,

Came across your repost of the Forbes piece on enterprise AI failure last month. The core argument tracks with what I've seen in production: the accuracy gap in RAG at scale sits in infrastructure, not prompt engineering. Data access patterns, retrieval architecture, latency under concurrent load.

I've been in that gap for two years. At Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from FAISS to Milvus, improved MRR from 45% to 95% after testing three vector databases on production-scale data. At Vivansh I built the same kind of system with 2-stage retrieval, 250+ per-query access control checks, vLLM-served inference, running at 500+ concurrent users on EC2.

Your work on observability and distributed databases is the infrastructure layer I want to work on next. If the Bengaluru team has room for a mid-level engineer, 15 minutes on Thursday would be worth it.

Amit

Varun Choudhary Software Engineer
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: varun.choudhary@promptql.io
IIT Madras grad building on PromptQL; PromptQL Program API (April 2026 launch) is the Python-based integration layer -- maps directly to Amit's FastAPI + LLM pipeline work
Initial Email
Subject: PromptQL Program API and Python pipeline work
Hi Varun,

Was reading through PromptQL's April announcement on the Program API. The Python-function integration approach is a clean abstraction: programs calling data functions, LLM primitives layered on top. Pretty close to the architecture I've been building production RAG systems on.

At Vivansh I built a multi-model orchestration pipeline: GPT-4o for document analysis, Qwen3-Coder-480B for blueprint generation, GPT-4o-mini for JSON conversion, all in FastAPI with Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas. Before that at Silver Touch I migrated a 50K-query-per-month RAG chatbot from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after benchmarking 6 embedding models and landing on BGE-M3 plus a reranker.

If PromptQL's engineering team has room for someone with this stack, would you know the right person to talk to?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-19
Subject: re: PromptQL Program API and Python pipeline work
Hi Varun,

Following up in case this got lost. One specific data point that might be relevant: on the form builder at Vivansh, GPT-4o and Claude both failed to reliably generate the platform's native JSON schema, but adding a Pydantic-validated intermediate step made it production-stable. That kind of abstraction is what I think makes the Program API approach durable at scale.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 2 sched 2026-05-26

amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Rohan K. Software Engineer
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: rohan@promptql.io
Bengaluru-based SWE building at PromptQL; the blog post 'Killing Slack was the only way to make AI accurate' (April 2026) captures what the core eng team is solving -- accuracy through structured data pipelines rather than prompt engineering
Initial Email
Subject: April post on Slack vs accurate AI
Hi Rohan,

Going through PromptQL's blog this week and the April post on Slack vs AI accuracy put words to something I keep running into in production: context fragmented across chat threads breaks retrieval accuracy. The systems that work are the ones where data structure and retrieval architecture match the actual query patterns.

I've spent two years building that kind of system. At Vivansh: a permission-aware RAG chatbot on Milvus, 10K+ content chunks, 2-stage retrieval with 250+ per-query access control checks, GPT-4o for query rewriting and answer generation, 80% MRR. At Silver Touch I rebuilt a 50K-queries/month RAG system from FAISS to Milvus, 45% to 95% MRR, after testing 6 embedding models on production data.

I'm based in Ahmedabad and tracking what PromptQL is building. If the Bengaluru team is hiring, happy to share more. Would you be open to pointing me in the right direction?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | github.com/amit-gohel | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-20
Subject: re: April post on Slack vs accurate AI
Hi Rohan,

Following up in case this got buried. Since my last note I read PromptQL's 'Your Homegrown AI Is Already Dead' post from last week. The 18-month failure pattern it describes matches what I saw at Silver Touch before the RAG infrastructure rebuild: the system worked in demos but fell apart on real query distributions. That's the problem I want to be working on.

Amit

Job Description
Software Engineer - PromptQL AI

About PromptQL
PromptQL is redefining how AI systems should access data in a business context with an emphasis on visibility, steer-ability, and repeatability. Building on the foundation of Hasura's Data Delivery Network (DDN), PromptQL is our flagship product. AI can't be trusted if it's confidently wrong. At PromptQL, we're on a mission to deliver reasoning AI that learns from enterprise data, signals its confidence, and earns trust through accuracy.

About the Role
As part of a small, high-impact team working directly with PromptQL leadership, you'll help shape the product direction and deliver core functionality across AI, data, and infrastructure layers.

What You'll Do
- Analyze the AI + data landscape to influence the evolution of PromptQL
- Translate feedback from sales and GTM teams into actionable product features
- Rapidly design and implement features across the PromptQL AI assistant, the PromptQL data engine, and the PromptQL runtime infrastructure

What We're Looking For
- Ability to understand and work across complex systems end-to-end
- Strong foundation in software engineering principles and adaptability across domains
- Proficiency in Python (PromptQL programs are Python; Primitives for classify, summarize, extract)
- Experience working with LLMs (OpenAI, Anthropic) -- big plus
- Experience with distributed data systems, query engines, or observability infrastructure

Tech Stack
- Python (primary programming language for PromptQL programs)
- LLM providers: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT models
- Hasura DDN connectors for enterprise data integration
- Runtime infrastructure

Location: Bengaluru, India
Compensation: 28-40 LPA

Outreach Angle

Amit's production Agentic RAG (LangChain + LangGraph, 50K+ queries/month, 95% MRR) maps directly to their JD ask for Agentic RAG pipelines and LLM orchestration for voice agents. Low-latency production experience (16s to 8s cut, 500+ concurrent users) aligns with their core technical challenge.

Notes

84+ employees. Co-founders: Mukesh Bansal + Abhishek Asawa (do NOT cold-email either â€” >50-person company rule). Key product: NuPlay voice AI. 1M+ calls handled. Deepgram event May 22 SF is a live hook.

Research Summary

Nurix AI builds enterprise voice + chat AI agents (NuPlay platform). Founded 2024, $42.2M raised (Accel, General Catalyst, Prosus). 84+ person team in Bengaluru. JD explicitly asks for Agentic RAG pipelines, LLM orchestration, low-latency production deployment â€” Amit's exact stack. Team is hands-on with multi-agent orchestration, voice AI evaluation, and production inference engineering. Email pattern: firstname.lastname@nurix.ai (59.3%).

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to mirror JD exact asks: agentic RAG pipelines (LangChain + LangGraph), multi-model LLM orchestration, low-latency inference (16s to 8s), Python/PyTorch/TF -- JD explicitly calls these out. Technical Skills reordered: LLMs and Techniques moved to first position since JD leads with LLM orchestration and Agentic RAG pipelines; LangChain/LangGraph promoted into that section; Languages now second with PyTorch and TensorFlow visible early (JD requires these explicitly). Hardware/IoT row dropped entirely -- JD is pure ML/voice AI, no embedded context. Voicer kept -- Samsung Solve for Tomorrow Top 10 among 70,000+ participants is a strong signal, and on-device inference relates to low-latency real-time ML. WeepScope added back as 2nd project: 1D CNN+GRU on audio signals for cry classification maps directly to Nurix voice/audio ML stack; bullet reframed to emphasize audio classification rather than hardware. All work experience bullets retained as-is.

Contacts & Emails 6 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 6 queued
Pushkar Patel ML Engineer
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: pushkar.patel@nurix.ai
Nurix featured Pushkar in their NEX video series on voice AI evaluation. He specifically argued that evaluating voice AI is fundamentally different from text LLMs -- latency, turn-taking, context retention, tone, recovery from failure, and behavior across multi-step workflows matter just as much as the final answer. He also reposted a paper on low-rank circuit conditioning for extracting and reusing neural network capabilities -- showing genuine interest in model internals and post-training. Background: Samsung Research, GSoC 2022, IIIT Vadodara. Working on inference engineering, kernels, agentic workflows, post-training at Nurix.
Initial Email
Subject: voice AI eval beyond the final answer
Hi Pushkar,

Caught your NEX video on voice AI evaluation a few weeks back. The argument you made -- that latency, turn-taking, and failure recovery are first-class evaluation signals, not just the final answer -- resonated. Most RAG evaluation I have seen in the wild still lives inside a text bubble.

I have been building production agentic RAG for the last two years: LangChain + LangGraph stateful orchestration handling permission checks, retrieval, tool calls, and answer generation in a single graph; 50K+ queries/month at 95% MRR after migrating off FAISS to Milvus; benchmarked 6 embedding models on live production data before settling on BGE-M3 with a reranker. The 'evaluate empirically on your actual distribution' instinct is the exact same loop I ran.

If your team is hiring on the ML side, I would be glad to talk. Does next Tuesday or Thursday work for 15 minutes?

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1 sched D+4
Subject: re: voice AI eval beyond the final answer
Hi Pushkar,

Following up in case this got buried. Shipped a short benchmark writeup on our 6-model embedding evaluation (BGE variants vs. Stella vs. SPLADE on production query data) -- happy to share if it is useful context. Either way, still interested in what your team is building on the inference and eval side at Nurix.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

Syed Amjad Ali Voice AI Engineer
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: amjad.ali@nurix.ai
Building Voice AI at Nurix. Previously at Akamai (CDN edge infrastructure, sub-50ms global delivery) and Razorpay (high-throughput distributed payment systems). His infra background in low-latency delivery maps directly to the real-time constraints of voice AI. PES University, Bengaluru.
Initial Email
Subject: Akamai edge instincts, voice AI latency
Hi Amjad,

Your background caught my eye -- Akamai edge infrastructure to voice AI at Nurix is a specific path. The sub-50ms delivery instincts from CDN work are a different mental model than most ML engineers bring to latency problems in real-time voice systems.

I have been on the infra side of ML: cut RAG response time from 16s to 8s under 500+ concurrent users by migrating vector infra from FAISS to Milvus, then layering query preprocessing with intent identification on top. The production pressure is similar -- you have a hard real-time budget and cannot afford to debug latency only in the happy path.

If Nurix is hiring on the voice AI or inference side, I would like to hear more about how the stack is built. Open to a 15-minute call next week if you have a slot.

Amit

Meghana Krishna AI Engineer
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: meghana.krishna@nurix.ai
Working on Nurix's sentinel layer for AI agent data privacy -- addressing how agent logs become PII black holes with SSNs, API secrets, and PHI cascading through chains. Nurix's approach uses NER plus regex techniques plus SHA-256 obfuscation. Security-conscious, privacy-first angle on production AI agents.
Initial Email
Subject: agent log PII and the sentinel layer
Hi Meghana,

Read about Nurix's sentinel layer for agent data privacy -- the framing of agent logs as PII black holes is accurate. SSNs and API secrets cascading through a multi-step chain before anyone notices is a real production problem, and the NER plus SHA-256 approach is the right direction.

I have been building production agentic pipelines with a similar awareness of what flows through the context: 250+ granular permissions per query enforced at retrieval time, with PostgreSQL as the ground truth for access control rather than trusting the LLM to self-filter. Milvus plus LangGraph handling the orchestration layer across 50K+ monthly queries.

If your team is hiring AI engineers, I'd be glad to talk. Open to a quick 15-minute call.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1 sched D+4

Job Description
Machine Learning Engineer (MLE) â€” Nurix

Key Responsibilities:

Speech and Voice Systems:
- Design, train, and optimize custom ASR/TTS models to overcome limitations of Sarvam ASR
- Develop agents handling interruptions, natural pauses, and real-world voice variability

Core ML/AI Development:
- Contribute to Voice-to-Voice (V2V) systems using state space models (e.g., Mamba, Hamba)
- Develop Agentic RAG pipelines and LLM orchestration for intelligent, context-aware voice agents
- Build self-learning agents for continuous improvement

Engineering and Deployment:
- Collaborate on bringing models into real-time, low-latency production environments
- Focus on scalability, security, and reliability

Collaboration and Research:
- Partner with product/engineering teams
- Stay current with ASR, TTS, NLP, and inference framework advances

Required Qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, or related field
- 5-10 years of hands-on ML engineering experience, with a focus on speech/NLP systems
- Expertise in ASR, TTS, NLP, LLM fine-tuning, or dialogue systems
- Proficiency in Python and ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX)
- Low-latency, real-time ML application design experience
- ML lifecycle, evaluation, and deployment knowledge
- Open-source contributions or research publications
- Prior multilingual or Indian-context ASR/TTS systems work preferred

Note: AI Engineer role also posted separately for 2-4 yrs experience, Bengaluru onsite. Other open roles (May 2026): Prompt Engineer (0-2 yrs), QA Engineer Conversational AI (3-5 yrs), Product Lead (8-10 yrs), Sr. DevOps (8 yrs), Enterprise Architect (10-15 yrs), Product Manager (4-6 yrs).

Notes

18+ roles on Naukri; ISO 42001 AI mgmt certified; strongest fintech OCR/doc-AI overlap. Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@perfios.com (confirmed nikita.b@perfios.com). 1K-5K employees. Clari5 is a Perfios subsidiary doing real-time fraud detection ML.

Research Summary

Perfios is India's largest B2B fintech SaaS company (1K-5K employees, Bengaluru HQ) processing bank statements, KYC, OCR documents for BFSI. Feb 2026: launched KScan AI (AI for 30M MSME credit assessment). Dec 2025: AI-Powered BFSI Operating System. Jan 2026: ISO 42001 AI certification (first BFSI company in India). Clari5 (subsidiary) actively scaling ML team for real-time fraud detection. 7 contacts mapped: Director Engineering Data Science (IIT KGP), Senior Manager Engineering, 3 AI/ML ICs, 2 TA recruiters. Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@perfios.com.

Tailoring Notes

1. Professional Summary: rewrote to lead with 'document AI, OCR pipelines, and ML systems' instead of generic RAG framing; added explicit mention of ~90% entity extraction on Aadhaar/PAN/GST and TensorFlow/PyTorch/Docker/AWS stack to mirror JD language. 2. Technical Skills reordering: promoted Python/ML frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn) to line 1; added a dedicated 'Document AI and OCR' category as line 2 (DocTR, DocLink, Tesseract, on-premise PDF pipelines, entity extraction) since that is Perfios's core product; moved Hardware/IoT bullet out and added Kafka to Infrastructure (explicitly required in JD for streaming ML). 3. Work Experience -- Silver Touch: moved the OCR pipeline bullet to position 1 (from position 4) because Aadhaar/PAN/GST entity extraction is the strongest match to Perfios Document AI; added 'zero cloud API calls, all on-premise' to highlight data sovereignty which Perfios cares about (ISO 42001, privacy-first messaging). 4. Projects and Certifications unchanged. Two-page layout maintained.

Contacts & Emails 7 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 7 queued
Anupam Mohanty Director Engineering - Data Science
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: anupam.m@perfios.com
Posted on LinkedIn recruiting Computer Vision engineers for his 'highly enthusiastic team of Data Scientists and challenging Computer Vision projects' -- used a custom CV test built by his team to fast-track screening. IIT Kharagpur alumni. He is the hiring manager for the Data Science group and is actively growing the team.
Initial Email
Subject: your CV hiring post and an OCR engineer
Hi Anupam,

Saw your LinkedIn post looking for CV engineers to join the data science team -- the custom screening test your team built was a nice touch.

I worked on a production OCR pipeline at Silver Touch for a client with strict data sovereignty requirements: all on-premise, 200+ page PDFs, entity extraction on Aadhaar, PAN, and GST numbers. The approach combined DocTR and DocLink (each did something the other missed), then used Nemotron-70b on a 250GB client server to merge outputs -- ended up at roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy. Before that I spent 5 months at IIT Delhi doing a systematic architecture search across 1,000+ CNN/BiLSTM configurations for a wearable gesture recognition system, so the model evaluation side is familiar territory too.

I am 2 years in on production AI and have been following what Perfios is building in document AI and the KScan AI launch. If your team is hiring on the ML side, I would love 15 minutes to hear how you think about the CV and document parsing work.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: bharat.r@perfios.com
Senior Manager Engineering at Perfios since 2018. Perfios just launched KScan AI (Feb 2026) -- AI that analyzes 30M Indian MSME profiles for credit and risk signals -- and the AI-Powered BFSI OS (Dec 2025). Both products require production ML engineering at scale, which is Bharat's domain.
Initial Email
Subject: KScan AI launch and a document AI engineer
Hi Bharat,

Went through Perfios's KScan AI release last month -- extracting credit and risk signals from 30M MSME profiles is a serious document parsing problem, and the ISO 42001 certification signals the kind of governance-first AI approach that makes on-prem pipelines matter.

Quick context on me: 2 years in production AI. At Silver Touch I built an on-premise OCR pipeline for sensitive government documents -- Aadhaar, PAN, GST numbers from 200+ page PDFs, using a hybrid DocTR + DocLink approach merged by an on-prem Nemotron-70b model, hitting roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy. Before that I rebuilt RAG infrastructure serving 50,000+ queries/month, migrating from FAISS to Milvus and improving MRR from 45% to 95% after benchmarking 6 embedding models.

If Perfios is hiring on the ML/document AI side, would value 15 minutes of your time to understand where the team is focused.

Amit

Initial Email
Subject: working on document AI at Perfios
Hi Sahil,

Came across your profile while looking into Perfios -- your work on NLP and Document AI is exactly the area I have been building in.

At Silver Touch I built an on-premise pipeline for sensitive government documents: combined DocTR and DocLink (each had different failure modes on layout vs. character accuracy), then used Nemotron-70b on the client's own server to merge the outputs. Ended up at roughly 90% entity extraction accuracy on Aadhaar, PAN, and GST numbers from 200+ page PDFs, with zero cloud API calls. I also fine-tuned embedding models and ran retrieval benchmarks (FAISS vs. Milvus vs. Qdrant) for a RAG system serving 50,000+ queries/month.

I am 2 years in on this kind of work and looking at roles where document parsing and NLP are the core problem. If Perfios is hiring on the ML side, would it make sense to connect? Even a quick note on how the team thinks about this would be helpful.

Amit

Job Description
Data Scientist - Time Series & Sequence Modeling (most relevant active Perfios listing on Naukri, Apr 2026; original AI/ML Engineer - Document AI listing has expired)

Must-Have:
- Strong background in Machine Learning, Deep Learning, and Statistical Modeling.
- Proficiency in Python and ML libraries (TensorFlow, PyTorch, Scikit-learn, XGBoost).
- Hands-on experience with time-series data, anomaly detection, or fraud detection.
- Strong feature engineering skills, especially with high-dimensional and noisy behavioral data.
- Knowledge of data processing frameworks (Spark, Kafka, Flink, etc.) for streaming/real-time data.
- Experience deploying models into production systems (ML Ops, APIs, containerized environments).

Role: Machine Learning Engineer
Industry: BPM/BPO / FinTech
Department: Data Science & Analytics
Key skills: TensorFlow, PyTorch, Time Series, Spark, Kafka, Sequence Modeling, Machine Learning
Location: Bengaluru / Mumbai / New Delhi
Experience: 3-5 years
Salary: 15-25 Lacs PA

Document AI context: Perfios core platform parses Aadhaar, PAN, bank statements, KYC, ITR for BFSI. KScan AI (Feb 2026) uses AI to assess 30M Indian MSMEs. ISO 42001 certified. Clari5 subsidiary does real-time ML fraud detection for banks.

Notes

AI Gateway processes 250B+ tokens. Cold email: '2 yrs RAG/FastAPI shipping production LLM apps.'

Research Summary

Portkey AI (~45 employees) builds an AI Gateway + LLMOps control plane processing 14B tokens/day across 24K customers. Raised $15M Series A (Feb 2026, Elevation + Lightspeed), then announced acquisition by Palo Alto Networks (Apr 30, 2026, close expected Q4 FY2026). Founders: Rohit Agarwal (CEO, @jumbld) and Ayush Garg (CTO). Engineering team in Bengaluru. Email pattern firstname@portkey.ai confirmed. Key recent signals: Portkey CLI launch (May 2026), Ayush's 'agent control is a gateway problem' thesis, acquisition announcement. Hiring may have slowed post-acquisition -- user should verify before sending.

Tailoring Notes

1. Summary: rewrote to call out LLMOps, multi-provider AI orchestration, VLLM model gateways, and LangChain/LangGraph -- mirrors Portkey's platform exactly. Dropped 'edge ML' and 'on-premise document processing' framing. 2. Technical Skills: reordered so LLMs and LLMOps comes first (multi-provider orchestration, VLLM, RAG, agentic AI, LangChain/LangGraph), then Vector DBs with embedding benchmarking emphasis, then Languages. Dropped Hardware/IoT line entirely -- not relevant to LLMOps backend role. 3. Vivansh bullets: brought multi-model orchestration (Form Builder: GPT-4o + Qwen3 + GPT-4o-mini) to the top -- directly relevant to Portkey's multi-provider gateway. Reframed to emphasize Pydantic-validated intermediate schema as structured output reliability pattern. 4. Silver Touch bullets: led with VLLM-served API gateway pattern (model decoupled from app, independent scaling, zero-downtime swaps) -- this is exactly what Portkey's Gateway does at scale. 5. Kept hardware project (Voicer) because Samsung Top 10 is a credibility signal, but its bullet is brief and below the fold.

Contacts & Emails 6 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 6 queued
Rohit Agarwal Co-founder and CEO
Founder queued LinkedIn
Email: rohit@portkey.ai
Posted April 30 about the PANW acquisition ('three years after founding' framing, 1.9K reactions). 2 weeks ago launched the Portkey CLI ('npx portkey setup'). 11 months ago wrote 'Why semantic caching is underused at Portkey' -- technical depth on caching LLM responses, directly relevant to Amit's latency work (16s to 8s reduction at Silver Touch).
Initial Email
Subject: went through your gateway docs after the CLI launch
Hi Rohit,

Went through the Portkey gateway docs after the CLI launch two weeks ago. Your semantic caching post from last year landed for me. We hit a similar latency wall at Silver Touch before moving from FAISS to Milvus, and the bottleneck turned out to be retrieval, not the LLM call. Caching at the right layer would have helped.

Quick context: I'm 2 years into production AI/ML. RAG at 95% MRR across 50K queries/month, multi-model orchestration with GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and GPT-4o-mini in one flow, and a VLLM model gateway at Silver Touch that decoupled the LLM from application code so we could swap providers without a redeploy. The VLLM work is the closest thing I've built to what Portkey does at scale.

If there's an open backend or LLMOps role, I'd love 15 minutes. Totally understand if the PANW timeline makes timing complicated right now.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-22
Subject: re: went through your gateway docs after the CLI launch
Hi Rohit,

Bumping this up in case it got buried. One more thought: the PANW announcement framed Portkey's value as 'AI agents are now privileged insiders.' That maps directly to the hardest problem I've worked on. I built a RAG system where 250+ permissions had to be enforced per query and caching was impossible because two users asking the same question legitimately get different answers. A control plane at the gateway layer would have simplified that significantly.

If now isn't the right window, happy to check back in a few weeks.

Amit
amitgohel.dev

Ayush Garg Co-founder and CTO
Founder queued LinkedIn
Email: ayush@portkey.ai
3 weeks ago posted 'Agent control is a gateway problem, not an orchestration problem' (60+ reactions) -- argues control (rate limits, fallbacks, guardrails) belongs at the gateway layer. 2 weeks ago posted about npx portkey setup CLI launch. Series A $15M announcement 2 months ago got 480+ reactions.
Initial Email
Subject: your post on gateway control vs orchestration
Hi Ayush,

Your post three weeks ago about agent control being a gateway problem landed for me. I ran into this building the Form Builder at Vivansh: GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder-480B, and GPT-4o-mini chained in one flow, and I was writing retry logic at the application layer for each handoff. Wrong place for it. Your point about centralizing control at the gateway is the cleaner design, and I learned it by doing it wrong first.

I'm 2 years into production AI/ML: RAG at 50K queries/month, multi-model orchestration with 3 providers in production, and a VLLM model gateway at Silver Touch that let us swap Llama-2-7b for Nemotron without touching the app. Looking at what's next, and Portkey's control plane work is the closest thing to what I'd want to build.

If you're hiring on the backend or LLMOps side, or there's someone on the eng team I should talk to, I'd appreciate a pointer. Open to a 15-min call next week.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

Subject: question about Portkey's forward deployed engineering
Hi Krishna,

Came across your profile when looking into Portkey's engineering team. Forward Deployed Engineer focused on agentic AI and LLMOps is a specific niche: working with enterprise customers to debug why their RAG pipeline dropped MRR after a data update, not just shipping features into a void.

That's most of what I've done the past 2 years. At Silver Touch: rebuilt RAG infra serving 50K queries/month, benchmarked 6 embedding models, and ended up at BGE-M3 with a reranker after FAISS at 45% MRR became the bottleneck (Milvus came out at 95%, Qdrant at 85% on the same production data). At Vivansh: permission-aware RAG for 500+ concurrent users (250+ permissions enforced per query, no caching possible) and recently moved the chatbot to a LangGraph-orchestrated agentic architecture with tool calling across 10+ platform APIs.

Job Description
Software Engineer (AI/Backend, LLMOps) -- Portkey, Bengaluru

Portkey is building a full-stack LLMOps platform that empowers AI builders to productionize their Gen AI apps reliably and securely.

Role: Software Engineer
Location: Bengaluru, India
Experience: 3-5 years backend development
Salary: 25-40 LPA

Requirements (extracted from Instahyre listing and platform context):
- 3-5 years of backend development experience
- Proficiency in Python, Node.js
- Proven experience in a startup environment, showcasing adaptability and agility
- Experience building LLMOps / AI infrastructure: gateway routing, observability, prompt management, guardrails
- Comfort working across providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, open-source LLMs)
- Production-grade systems thinking: reliability, latency, cost optimization across LLM calls

Platform context:
- AI Gateway: routes LLM calls across 250+ providers with fallbacks, load balancing, caching
- Observability: real-time monitoring, logging, analytics for LLM usage
- Prompt management: versioning, A/B testing, deployment pipelines
- Guardrails and governance: content filtering, PII detection, policy enforcement
- RAG playground and evaluation frameworks

(Note: full JD text unavailable -- Instahyre required login. Reconstructed from Google snippet and Portkey public product documentation.)

Postman

Bengaluru · Applied AI Engineer, Postbot (2+ yrs)

RoleApplied AI Engineer, Postbot (2+ yrs)
Salary25-35 LPA
Hiringactively hiring

Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Postbot is Postman's flagship GenAI assistant; team hires 2-5 yr full-stack engineers for LLM/agentic workflows; FastAPI/Python/Node stack aligns with Amit's RAG/LLM background.

Notes

Bengaluru HQ; 2-5 yr band confirmed on Weekday job board. Cold email: emphasize Postbot prompt orchestration experience + 50K queries/month production RAG.

Research Summary

Postman launched v12 (~2 months ago) - an AI-native platform repositioning from API client to agentic workflow infrastructure. Agent Mode (co-founder Abhijit Kane building it) reviews API specs, flags breaking changes, and auto-generates migration guides - the same LLM-over-structured-context pattern Amit runs in production (Form Builder: 3-LLM with Pydantic schemas; RAG: 50K queries/month). Kane wrote 1 month ago inviting people interested in agent evolution to reach out. 8 contacts mapped (per >50-person company rule): 2 Tier 4 recruiters (Israel Shaik, Navitha Pereira), 2 Tier 1 EMs (Rajesh K. - no email, Hemendra Kumar), 3 Tier 3 AI engineers (Hasnain, Praveen, Gitesh), 1 Tier 2 co-founder kept (Kane - explicitly invited outreach). Email pattern: first.last@postman.com (unverified). Original Postbot JD URL expired but 115 open roles remain.

Tailoring Notes

2-page format -- all work experience, projects, and skills preserved. Summary rewritten to lead with 'production LLM and agentic systems' and call out Pydantic-validated multi-model orchestration + Milvus retrieval, mirroring Postman v12 Agent Mode architecture. Skills reordered: LLM and Agentic AI moved to first category to match JD emphasis on 'agentic era', 'AI-native'; Vector Databases kept second. All 5 Vivansh bullets kept including video course pipeline. IIT Delhi restored (3 bullets). Projects section restored: Voicer (2 bullets, Samsung Solve for Tomorrow hook) + WeepScope. Smart India Hackathon 2023 restored to Certifications. Hardware/IoT skills line restored. Key JD phrases driving summary/skills ordering: 'agentic era', 'AI-native', 'structured output', 'agent sandboxes, programmatic tools', 'agentic workflow production experience'.

Subject: agentic AI work, interested in Postbot team
Hi Rajesh,

Saw you lead the Agentic AI and Gen AI Platforms team at Postman. I've been following Agent Mode since Abhijit Kane's post about the spec review pipeline. The problem of running an LLM over structured context and generating validated structured output is close to what I built at Vivansh.

That system used GPT-4o for PDF analysis, Qwen3-Coder-480B for blueprint generation (it won the benchmark over GPT-4o on this task), and GPT-4o-mini for final JSON conversion, all tied through a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema. Before that, at Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure from 45% to 95% MRR at 50K+ queries/month after benchmarking Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on production data and selecting BGE-M3 from 6 embedding models.

If your team is hiring on the applied AI side, I'd welcome 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the Agent Mode architecture. amitgohel.dev for context.
