github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-30
Subject: re: agentic AI work, interested in Postbot team
Hi Rajesh,

Following up on this. One thing I thought might be relevant to Agent Mode's architecture: in my RAG system, the permission-filtering layer (dedicated Milvus index + PostgreSQL cross-reference) is structurally separate from the content retrieval layer. That separation keeps access control logic out of the content index and avoids re-indexing when permissions change. I suspect Agent Mode has a similar challenge with spec scoping per user or workspace.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat

Hemendra Kumar Engineering Manager at Postman, Building & Scaling High-Throughput Systems
EM/Lead sent LinkedIn
Email: hemendra.kumar@postman.com
EM at Postman building and scaling high-throughput systems - the infrastructure challenge under the Postbot AI platform. His background in building/scaling matches the scale Amit has worked at (50K queries/month RAG, AWS Lambda pipelines, on-premise LLM serving).
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: scaling AI pipelines, question for Postman EM
Hi Hemendra,

Going through Postman's engineering team this week and noticed your focus on building and scaling high-throughput systems. The infrastructure challenge under an LLM-heavy product like Postbot is something I've been in the middle of.

At Silver Touch I moved an in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API, replaced FAISS with Milvus, and cut response time from 16s to 8s while handling 50K+ queries/month across 10+ chatbot instances. The core lesson: model serving and retrieval need independent scaling paths or any traffic spike creates a compound failure. At Vivansh the same pattern came up with bulk LLM processing (100+ PDFs in parallel), Pydantic validation as a safety layer, and AWS Lambda for burst rendering.

If Postman is hiring on the applied AI side and you're involved in those conversations, I'd be glad to have 15 minutes. Resume at amitgohel.dev.

Amit

I work on hybrid and semantic retrieval in production. Generating typed SDK wrappers from API specs through an LLM is a structured-output problem I've been working on from a different angle: form specs to platform JSON. The core challenge is identical (LLMs are unreliable on deeply nested output until you give them a simpler intermediate representation to target).

I've spent 2 years on production LLM systems: RAG at 50K+ queries/month, a 3-LLM form builder with Pydantic-validated intermediate schemas, agentic orchestration with LangChain and LangGraph. Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: taking you up on the agent evolution invite
Hi Abhijit,

Saw your post a month ago about Agent Mode. At the end you wrote that if you've been following themes in agent evolution (agent sandboxes, programmatic tools, filesystem-based data access) and are looking to build in that zone, to reach out. So here I am.

The multi-LLM form builder I built at Vivansh is structurally close to Agent Mode's approach. PDF spec comes in, GPT-4o analyzes it, Qwen3-Coder-480B generates a structured blueprint (it beat GPT-4o on this task in testing), GPT-4o-mini converts it to the platform's native JSON through a Pydantic-validated intermediate schema. The insight was the same one you're working with: LLMs struggle on deeply nested output, but give them a simpler intermediate representation and validate with Pydantic, and it becomes reliable. Before that, at Silver Touch I rebuilt RAG infrastructure serving 50K+ queries/month, improving MRR from 45% to 95% after benchmarking Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on production data.

Not sure if there's an opening or if this is the right moment. Taking you up on the invitation seemed worth a shot. Resume and context at amitgohel.dev.

Storing specs, tests, and mocks together and having AI coordinate changes across all three is a distributed state problem I find genuinely interesting. The permission-aware RAG I built has a similar coordination challenge: permission index, content index, and PostgreSQL user state all need to stay consistent as permissions change, without re-indexing the content.

Job Description
Applied AI Engineer, Postbot - Postman (Bengaluru, India)

Note: Original JD URL (boards.greenhouse.io/postman/jobs/5554642003) expired. Reconstructed from CSV metadata + Postman v12 product context + related active Bengaluru JD (Senior Engineer, Search - job-boards.greenhouse.io/postman/jobs/7701509003).

Role: Applied AI Engineer, Postbot (2+ years experience)
Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
In-office: 3 days/week (moving to 5 days by year-end)
Salary: 25-35 LPA

What you will build:
- Postbot: Postman's AI assistant for writing tests, understanding APIs, generating code inside the Postman workspace
- Agent Mode: AI that reviews API specs, flags breaking changes, auto-generates v2 specs, migration guides, and Postman collections
- AI-native features across the Postman v12 platform - specs, tests, mocks, SDK generation all touched by AI
- Playwright plugin and CLI integrations

Tech stack (confirmed from related Bengaluru JD):
- Python and JavaScript primary
- ElasticSearch / OpenSearch for AI-powered search
- Apache Kafka, Kinesis for streaming
- LLMs in the loop for query translation and structured output generation
- Agentic workflows, API spec analysis, structured output (JSON/YAML)

Likely requirements:
- 2-5 years experience
- Strong Python + LLM/agentic workflow production experience
- RAG, retrieval, or agent orchestration in production
- FastAPI or similar REST API development
- Experience shipping AI features used by real developers at scale

Culture:
- 40 million developer users
- Forbes Cloud 100 for 6 consecutive years
- Postman v12 is their biggest product bet since founding
- New Bengaluru office (5 months old), active India QBR, CSR programs
- Compensation: salary + comprehensive benefits + flexible PTO

AI-first digital engineering firm; explicit RAG/LLM/Agentic frameworks (LangGraph, LangChain, AWS Bedrock Agentcore); Elite NVIDIA/AWS/GCP partner; SageMaker/Lambda stack.

Notes

Multiple Bengaluru GenAI MLE postings; Levels.fyi MLE total comp ₹8.8L-22.7L+ mid-level; senior 25-35 LPA.

Research Summary

Quantiphi (1K-5K employees, Bengaluru HQ for India) is an AI-first digital engineering firm. Phi Labs is their R&D hub — the division behind the open JR9410 Associate Architect - ML Engineer role. The role sits at the intersection of GenAI research and production architecture: RAG, multi-agent systems, LLM reasoning, evaluation/benchmarking. Recent signals: AWS GenAI Innovation Center First Preferred Partner (April 2026), Candyspace acquisition (March 2026), Phi Labs launch (May 2025). Email pattern: firstname.lastname@quantiphi.com. 8 contacts mapped across tiers 1/3/4.

Tailoring Notes

1. Professional Summary rewritten to mirror JD language: 'intersection of research and scalable architecture design', 'LangGraph-orchestrated agentic systems', explicit benchmarking numbers (FAISS vs Milvus vs Qdrant, 6 embedding models, 45% to 95% MRR) — maps to JD's 'run benchmarks and evaluations' requirement. 2. Technical Skills reordered: AI/ML Frameworks (LangChain, LangGraph) moved to first bullet since Phi Labs role centers on agent orchestration frameworks; Vector DBs second; Hardware/IoT bullet removed entirely (pure software/GenAI role). 3. WeepScope project removed — hardware-only, no GenAI relevance to this role. Voicer kept because Samsung Solve for Tomorrow achievement adds credibility. IIT Delhi research kept — maps to Phi Labs' research-to-production theme.

Contacts & Emails 8 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 8 queued
Lalit Gupta Enterprise AI/ML Architect | GenAI Platform Builder at Quantiphi
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: lalit.gupta@quantiphi.com
Feb 2026 LinkedIn post arguing 'guardrails are the real architecture, not the model' — discussed production GenAI requiring orchestration layers, RAG pipelines, vector DBs, moderation filters, model routing, cost governance. Also argued for modularity: if the architecture can't swap models without a redesign, you built a solution not a platform. Both points map directly to Amit's LangGraph state machine (explicit nodes for permission filtering, retrieval, tool-calling, answer generation) and the FAISS-to-Milvus migration work.
Initial Email
Subject: your architecture-not-model post, a quick question
Saw your February post where you argued that guardrails are the real architecture, not the model. The point about modularity stuck: if swapping models requires redesigning the system, you built a solution instead of a platform.

That's almost word-for-word how I framed the LangGraph system I built at Vivansh. Explicit graph nodes for permission filtering, retrieval, tool-calling, and answer synthesis. Swapping GPT-4o for Qwen3-Coder-480B in the form pipeline was a one-config change. Same principle drove the RAG rebuild: migrated from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR went from 45% to 95% on 50,000+ queries per month.

Applying for the JR9410 Associate Architect role in Phi Labs and wanted to reach out directly. Would a 15-minute call work next Tuesday or Thursday?

Amit Gohel
Job Description
Associate Architect - Machine Learning Engineer
JR9410 | IN KA Bengaluru | Full time | Posted 16 Days Ago | End Date: May 30, 2026

While technology is the heart of our business, a global and diverse culture is the heart of our success. We love our people and we take pride in catering them to a culture built on transparency, diversity, integrity, learning and growth.

If working in an environment that encourages you to innovate and excel, not just in professional but personal life, interests you- you would enjoy your career with Quantiphi!

About Phi Labs

Phi Labs is the innovation and research division of Quantiphi, focused on advancing next-generation AI technologies and translating cutting-edge research into scalable enterprise solutions. The team works on emerging areas including Generative AI, autonomous AI agents, multimodal intelligence, and AI platform innovation.

Role Overview:

We are looking for a Machine Learning Solution Architect who will operate at the intersection of AI research, advanced prototyping, and scalable architecture design.

This role will focus on exploring, experimenting, and productionizing cutting-edge AI techniques, particularly in Generative AI and Agentic AI systems. The architect will work closely with research scientists, applied ML engineers, and platform teams to design novel AI capabilities, validate them through rapid experimentation, and evolve them into reusable AI platforms and frameworks.

Key Responsibilities:

AI Research & Innovation

Explore and evaluate state-of-the-art research in Generative AI, LLM architectures, and autonomous AI agents.

Prototype novel AI capabilities and system designs based on emerging research.

Contribute to internal research initiatives, whitepapers, and innovation accelerators.

Translate academic or open research ideas into enterprise-ready AI architectures.

Ability to interpret and implement research papers across multiple domains, including Life Sciences and Digital Twins.

Strong understanding of DevOps practices such as containerization, scalable deployments, and production-ready infrastructure.

Up-to-date with recent advancements and trends in Generative AI.

Generative AI & Agentic AI Systems:

Design architectures for agentic AI systems capable of planning, reasoning, and tool usage.

Build experimental frameworks for:
Multi-agent collaboration
Autonomous decision-making workflows
LLM reasoning and planning systems
Retrieval-augmented AI systems

Explore innovations in AI memory systems, tool orchestration, and knowledge grounding.

Rapid Prototyping & Experimental Platforms

Lead development of research prototypes and experimental AI platforms.

Develop reference architectures for GenAI applications such as:
AI copilots
AI research assistants
Autonomous workflow agents

Run benchmarks and evaluations for new models, architectures, and agent frameworks.

AI Platform & Architecture Design

Define modular AI architectures that support experimentation and rapid iteration.

Architect internal platforms that enable fast development of GenAI and agent-based systems.

Apply platform engineering principles to build reusable AI components including:
agent orchestration layers
prompt management systems
evaluation frameworks
experimentation infrastructure.

Collaboration with Research & Engineering Teams

Work closely with ML engineers to convert research concepts into working prototypes.

Provide technical leadership for AI architecture and system design decisions.

Guide engineering teams in building scalable implementations of experimental AI systems.

Required Qualifications:

5-7+ years of experience in Machine Learning, AI engineering, or AI system architecture
Strong experience with deep learning and large language models
Experience building LLM-based applications or frameworks
Solid programming expertise in Python
Experience designing distributed AI systems
Strong understanding of ML system architecture and productionization

Preferred Experience:

Experience with agent frameworks and autonomous AI systems
Experience building RAG pipelines and knowledge-driven AI systems
Familiarity with research papers and experimental AI frameworks
Experience with AI evaluation, benchmarking, and model experimentation
Experience with cloud-native architectures and platform engineering

Technologies & Areas of Exploration:

Candidates should have familiarity or interest in areas such as:
LLM ecosystems
Autonomous AI agents
AI orchestration frameworks
Vector databases and knowledge retrieval
Multimodal AI systems
AI reasoning and planning architectures

Match Reason

JD explicitly lists FastAPI, LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, LangGraph, RAG, vector DBs, multi-agent — near-perfect overlap with Amit's RAG/LLM/FastAPI/production AI stack. India's sovereign LLM flagship.

Notes

Govt-selected for India sovereign LLM; $53M Series A (Lightspeed/Peak XV/Khosla). 24+ open roles. Cold email: lead with production RAG deployments + FastAPI at scale + multilingual interest.

Research Summary

Sarvam AI is India's govt-selected sovereign LLM company ($53M Series A, Lightspeed/Peak XV/Khosla). The FDSE role spans 4 product teams; ARYA is the primary fit - it's their agent orchestration stack built on 8 composable primitives, immutable state ledger, and declarative HCL authoring. Amit's LangGraph multi-agent RAG chatbot (50K queries/month, 250+ permissions, multi-tenant) maps directly to what ARYA is solving at production scale. Email pattern confirmed as firstname@sarvam.ai. Key contacts: Aalekh Sharan (Head Agentic AI, Tier 1), Vivek Raghavan + Pratyush Kumar (Co-Founders, Tier 2), 3 FDSEs on the same team (Tier 3). Recent hooks: Aalekh on Tech-à-Tête podcast April 2026; Open-source 30B/105B March 2026; Arya blog Feb 2026.

Tailoring Notes

1. Summary rewritten to lead with LangGraph + multi-agent (JD: 'multi-agent architectures, memory management, orchestration') and MRR numbers; removed 'edge ML' entirely (not relevant for ARYA/FDSE). 2. Skills reordered: Programming Languages first (JD: 'strong Python fluency'); AI/ML Frameworks second with LangGraph added explicitly (JD bonus: 'LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, LangGraph'); new 'Agentic AI and RAG' line added for tool calling + eval design (JD requirements); Hardware/IoT line dropped entirely (not relevant). 3. Vivansh bullet 1: LangGraph made explicit as 'stateful multi-agent graph' (mirrors JD ARYA team description). 4. IIT Delhi work experience removed (hardware/wearable research not relevant for pure software AI role; kept 1000+ model eval credibility in edge). 5. Projects section (Voicer + WeepScope) removed; Samsung Top 10 moved to Certifications as one-liner. 6. Margins tightened to 0.9cm top/bottom to achieve one-page fit.

Contacts & Emails 8 contacts · 8 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 0 queued
Aalekh Sharan Head, Agentic AI & Head, Public Sector
EM/Lead sent LinkedIn
Email: aalekh@sarvam.ai
Appeared on Tech-à-Tête podcast Episode 2 (~April 2026) discussing building AI that is safe, sovereign, and scalable - specifically multilingual model challenges and compute constraints for production agentic systems. Also spoke at TEDxBITS (The Odyssey). As Head of Agentic AI, directly leads the ARYA team that Amit is best suited for.
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: question about ARYA's production agent stack
Aalekh,

Listened to your Tech-à-Tête episode last month. The bit about compute constraints forcing you to rethink how agents actually behave under production load was what made me go deep on the ARYA blog post after.

Quick context on why I'm writing: I spent the last year upgrading an EHS chatbot to a LangGraph stateful multi-agent architecture: 250+ permission checks per query, 500+ concurrent users, Milvus backend. Before that, a FAISS to Milvus migration brought MRR from 45% to 95% after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data. The constraint-as-design-principle framing in the ARYA post maps pretty directly to what we hit trying to make permission-aware retrieval reliable at scale.

I'm applying for the FDSE role on the ARYA team. If you have 15 minutes next Tuesday or Wednesday, I'd like to hear how the ARYA primitives hold up under multi-tenant load.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-17
Subject: re: question about ARYA's production agent stack
Aalekh,

Following up in case this got buried. Since I wrote last week, put up a short write-up on amitgohel.dev on the permission-aware RAG architecture; the part about separating the permission index from the content index is what I think maps closest to ARYA's ledger scoping. Happy to share it if useful.

Amit
Follow-up 2 sched 2026-05-23
Subject: re: question about ARYA's production agent stack
Aalekh,

Last note on this. Saw Sarvam's ASR evaluation post from April. The LLM-WER and intent scoring framing reminded me of the eval pipelines we built for the agentic chatbot. If the FDSE role or timing works better later, I'd still appreciate being on your radar.

Amit

EM/Lead sent LinkedIn
Email: tushar@sarvam.ai
Leads Edge AI and GTM - relevant to Samvaad/API Dashboard teams. Sarvam Edge launched Feb 14, 2026. ex-McKinsey + Google AI4SG background; IIT Kanpur.
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: Sarvam Edge, ARYA, and the FDSE opening
Tushar,

Saw the Sarvam Edge announcement in February. On-device inference at scale with the constraint of Indian hardware budgets is a genuinely different problem than deploying a quantized model and calling it done.

Context: I'm applying for the FDSE role at Sarvam. The last two years have been production RAG infrastructure: FAISS to Milvus migration (MRR 45% to 95%), LangGraph multi-agent upgrade handling 250+ permission checks per query, multi-model orchestration across GPT-4o, Gemini, and Qwen3. The API Dashboard and ARYA tracks on the FDSE spec match the kind of work I've been doing.

Would you be open to 15 minutes this week? Wanted to hear how the edge and cloud inference tracks at Sarvam divide the problem space.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-17
Subject: re: Sarvam Edge, ARYA, and the FDSE opening
Tushar,

Amit
Follow-up 2 sched 2026-05-23
Subject: re: Sarvam Edge, ARYA, and the FDSE opening
Tushar,

Amit
Vivek Raghavan Co-Founder
Founder sent LinkedIn
Email: vivek@sarvam.ai
Co-founded Sarvam with mission 'GENAI for a Billion Indians.' Open-sourced Sarvam 30B and 105B on March 6, 2026 - India's largest open-source LLM release. The scale of production inference challenges at 30B/105B maps to Amit's multi-model orchestration work.
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: thoughts on the 30B open-source release
Vivek,

Followed the 30B and 105B open-source release in March, specifically the production inference choices at that scale. Serving 30B at reasonable latency for Indian enterprise loads is a different problem than serving 7B, and the decision to release both weights and checkpoints was notable.

Context: I spent the last year at Vivansh rebuilding RAG infrastructure serving 50K+ queries/month: FAISS to Milvus migration brought MRR from 45% to 95%, LangGraph multi-agent upgrade with 250+ permission checks per query. The orchestration layer ended up handling GPT-4o, Gemini 2.5, and Qwen3 in the same pipeline. Working against Sarvam's own models would be a genuinely interesting set of problems.

I'm applying for the FDSE role, ARYA track. If a 20-minute call makes sense, I'm free most mornings next week.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-17
Subject: re: thoughts on the 30B open-source release
Vivek,

Following up from last week. Put a short project write-up on amitgohel.dev; the multi-tenant permission-aware RAG section is probably the most relevant to what ARYA is solving at production scale. Happy to walk through it if useful.

Pratyush Kumar Co-Founder

Authored the ARYA technical blog post (Feb 10, 2026) - immutable state ledger, 8 composable primitives, declarative HCL authoring, Acta Diurna tracing. The constraint 'same model + same task, one approach collapsed under context, the other finished - difference wasn't intelligence, it was structure' maps directly to Amit's production RAG optimization work.
Initial Email sent 2026-05-14
Subject: question about ARYA's immutable state ledger
Pratyush,

Read the ARYA post when it came out in February and came back to it a couple times since. The observation about context collapse: same model, same task, two approaches, only one survives production. That's a failure mode I've hit and didn't have a clean framework for until your post.

Background: I've been building production RAG and agentic systems for two years. At Vivansh, upgrading to a LangGraph stateful multi-agent architecture with 250+ permission checks per query was specifically about the structure you describe. Before that, the FAISS to Milvus migration was driven by the same idea: retrieval reliability isn't a model problem, it's a pipeline design problem.

I'm applying for the FDSE role on the ARYA team. Would you have 15 minutes to talk through how the ledger model holds up when task graphs get deeply nested?

Amit
amitgohel.dev
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-17
Subject: re: question about ARYA's immutable state ledger

Job Description
ENGINEERING
Forward Deployed Software Engineer | 2-5 years
Engineering | Bengaluru | Full Time | On-Site

About Sarvam
Sarvam is building the bedrock of Sovereign AI for India. The company is developing India's full-stack sovereign AI platform, building across research, models, infrastructure and applications with a singular focus on making AI genuinely work for India. Sarvam works with leading enterprises and public institutions and is backed by Lightspeed, Peak XV, and Khosla Ventures. Sarvam partners with India's leading brands, including Tata Capital, SBI Life, CRED, IDFC, and LIC.

About the Role
We are looking for high-agency engineers who can architect and build production-grade solutions using generative AI and agentic systems. As an FDSE at Sarvam, you will work directly with customers and product teams, understand their problems deeply, and ship real solutions fast - not prototypes or demos, but things that run in production.

This is not a single opening. We have multiple positions across Samvaad, API Dashboard, Content Studio, and ARYA - and we will match you to the team where your skills and interests fit best.

What You'll Do

Samvaad: Build, deploy, monitor, and evaluate conversational AI agents across channels - Voice, WhatsApp, website (voice and chat), and in-app - integrating them deeply into client systems and workflows. Scope and design end-to-end technical solutions for client requirements. Own client engagements from kickoff to go-live. Instrument and improve deployed agents: debug failure modes, tune prompts, and iterate on agent behaviour based on real usage data.

API Dashboard: Build plugins, integrations, and developer-facing tooling. Contribute to open-source projects. Own the developer experience end-to-end.

Content Studio: Own end-to-end deployment of Sarvam's dubbing and live translation APIs into client and partner production environments. Serve as primary technical point of contact with clients.

ARYA: Build MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers and agentic backend infrastructure. Apply strong system design thinking to architect multi-agent systems: tool calling, memory, orchestration, and inter-agent coordination. Design and maintain data pipelines using messaging systems like Kafka, SQS, or RabbitMQ. Work with SQL and NoSQL databases at scale. Work with agentic frameworks like LangGraph and Google ADK. Contribute to internal tooling and infrastructure.

What We're Looking For
- 2-5 years of software engineering experience with a track record of building and shipping production systems
- Strong Python fluency and comfort across the stack
- Deep understanding of how LLMs and agents work - context windows, tokens, embeddings, RAG, tool calling, prompt design, and failure modes; hands-on experience with both open and closed-source LLMs
- Experience designing and implementing multi-agent architectures, including memory management and orchestration
- Ability to set up eval pipelines and define meaningful quality metrics for agentic AI systems
- Systems thinking: ability to reason about architecture, reliability, scale, trade-offs, and failure modes
- High agency - you unblock yourself, figure things out, and ship without hand-holding

Bonus Points
- End-to-end deployment of working AI agents in production
- Side projects, open-source contributions, or products shipped outside of work
- Prior experience in a customer-facing or product-embedded engineering role
- Deep familiarity with LLM orchestration frameworks (LangChain, LlamaIndex, CrewAI, LangGraph)
- Experience with voice/speech models (TTS, STT, ASR), cloud platforms, or real-time media and streaming pipelines
- Familiarity with Sarvam models and APIs from personal or project use

Swiggy

Bengaluru · Machine Learning Engineer II
done
LocationBengaluru
RoleMachine Learning Engineer II
Salary20-35 LPA
Hiringactively hiring
Priority96
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Explicit MLE II posting requires 2-4 yrs Python/TF/Keras; GenAI search and personalization initiatives; Bolt 10-min delivery uses ML for ETA/dispatch.

Notes

Strong public eng blog (bytes.swiggy.com); IPO'd 2024 → ESOP upside. 13+ open ML roles.

Research Summary

Swiggy (10K+ employees, IPO'd Nov 2024) is aggressively expanding its AI layer: MCP server launched May 2026, Sarvam AI partnership for multilingual voice commerce announced March 2026, Builders Club opening the AI commerce stack to developers. The MLE II role requires 2-4 years productionizing ML in high-throughput/low-latency settings using Python, Spark, TensorFlow, PyTorch, AWS, and Kubernetes. Amit's RAG pipeline (7-domain, Milvus, 60B-param LLM) and multi-model orchestration work (Claude/GPT-4o routing) map directly to their GenAI search and agent infrastructure direction. 8 contacts identified: 3 senior ML (Tier 1), 2 junior/mid DS (Tier 3), 3 tech recruiters (Tier 4). Email pattern: first.last@swiggy.in.

Tailoring Notes

1. Summary rewritten to mirror JD language: replaced 'edge ML' with 'high-throughput, low-latency settings' (verbatim JD phrase), front-loaded Python/TF/Keras/PyTorch/AWS/LangChain to match Swiggy stack, added '500+ concurrent users' and '50,000+ queries/month' to hit the scale signal the JD demands. 2. Technical Skills: reordered ML frameworks row to put TensorFlow/Keras and PyTorch before Hugging Face (JD emphasizes TF/Keras/PyTorch over transformers); renamed second category to 'ML and AI' to surface RAG, Agentic AI, CNN/LSTM prominently; dropped Hardware/IoT row entirely (pure software/GenAI role, IoT is noise). 3. Projects: dropped Voicer and WeepScope hardware projects -- they are TinyML/embedded and do not help for an ML platform/GenAI role; removing them tightens the one-page layout and keeps the focus on production ML. 4. All three work experience roles and IIT Delhi bullets kept -- Silver Touch RAG (50K queries, MRR 111% improvement, latency -50%) and Vivansh agentic LangGraph system both map directly to Swiggy's ML at scale and GenAI search focus.

Contacts & Emails 8 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 8 queued
Srinivas N Director - Data Science
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: srinivas.n@swiggy.in
Leads search and recommendations ML at Swiggy; the Sarvam AI partnership for multilingual voice commerce will dramatically expand the retrieval and ranking surface -- Amit's RAG pipeline work with Milvus across 7 domains for a 60B-param LLM maps directly to that next scaling challenge.
Initial Email
Subject: Swiggy search stack after the Sarvam tie-up
Hi Srinivas,

Came across your work on Swiggy's search and recommendations stack, then saw the Sarvam AI partnership from March. Multilingual voice queries are going to hit retrieval differently than typed search, and I've been thinking about how ranking holds up when query structure shifts that much.

Quick context: I spent the past year building production RAG at Vivansh, 2-stage retrieval over Milvus with a permission index cross-referencing PostgreSQL, running at 80% MRR on 1,000+ queries/day. Before that at Silver Touch I migrated a 50K-query-per-month chatbot from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR went from 45% to 95%. The retrieval quality side is what I keep going back to.

If your team is hiring on the ML side, I'd be glad for a 15-minute call to hear how you're thinking about the retrieval layer post-Sarvam. No pressure either way.

Amit

Darpan Chanana Associate Data Scientist
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: darpan.chanana@swiggy.in
Posted last month about Maximum Marginal Relevance in RAG pipelines (reducing retrieved-document redundancy) and Hybrid Search in RAG (dense + sparse retrieval for better LLM context) -- Amit built a 7-domain RAG system with Milvus using these exact techniques for a 60B-param production LLM.
Initial Email
Subject: your MMR and hybrid search RAG posts
Hi Darpan,

Read both your posts from last month, the one on Maximum Marginal Relevance in RAG and the hybrid search one. The point about MMR trading off relevance for diversity in the retrieved set is exactly the tradeoff I kept hitting when building multi-domain RAG at Silver Touch. We ended up at BGE-M3 with a reranker after testing 6 embedding models on production data, which helped, but the diversity problem resurfaced once query volume hit 50K+ a month.

I'm 2 years into production RAG and retrieval work, most recently at Vivansh where I built a 2-stage system with Milvus handling 10,000+ chunks across multiple knowledge sources and 750+ course assignments per user. The retrieval quality side is what I keep going back to.

If Swiggy is hiring on the ML or data science side, you'd probably be a better judge of who I should talk to. Worth connecting?

Amit

Job Description
Machine Learning Engineer II -- Swiggy, Bengaluru

Experience: 2-4 years (minimum 2+ years productionizing ML models in high-throughput, low-latency settings)
Skills filter: Data Science / Machine Learning, Data Mining, Java, Python, Spark, SQL, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Keras, ONNX, AWS, GCP, Azure, Kubernetes, ETL pipelines, LangChain (plus)

About Swiggy: India's largest on-demand delivery platform. Tech-first approach. 500+ cities, 5000+ employees, 2 lakh+ delivery fleet. Processes terabytes of data daily using ML for delivery ETA, personalization, search, and recommendations.

Role overview: The MLE II will join Swiggy's ML engineering team contributing to ML-driven solutions. Ideal candidate is familiar with ML fundamentals, algorithms, OOP. Must be proficient in Python, Spark, ETL pipelines, and cloud provider stacks (AWS/Azure/GCP). Expertise in Kubernetes, TensorFlow, and PyTorch is highly desired.

Key responsibilities:
- Deploy and maintain ML models on Swiggy's in-house ML platform and AWS at scale
- Build and optimize ETL pipelines for large-scale data processing
- Collaborate with data science team to drive decision-making focused on scale, latency, and throughput
- Develop tooling and systems to accelerate the ML lifecycle
- Enable sophisticated algorithmic strategies for data mining and decision-making
- Contribute to data-driven products across Swiggy (ETA prediction, GenAI search, personalization, recommendations)

Must-have skills: Python, Spark, SQL, cloud (AWS primary), Kubernetes, TensorFlow/PyTorch
Nice-to-have: ONNX, LangChain, Scala
Focus areas: Bolt (10-min delivery ETA), GenAI search and personalization, ML platform reliability

Notes

Research Summary

Tredence is building an agentic analytics platform (root cause analysis, hypothesis testing, question expansion, automated reporting) using LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, Databricks, Docker, Kubernetes, Azure. Role is Senior AI/ML Engineer focused on multi-agent system design and event-driven pipeline architecture. Company recently won Google Cloud Partner of Year 2026 (Retail) and ISG Databricks Leader 2026. 1K-5K employees. 7 contacts found across Tier 1 (2 senior AI/ML), Tier 3 (2 IC ML engineers), Tier 4 (3 TA/recruiters). Email pattern: firstname.lastinitial@tredence.com.

Tailoring Notes

Hi Aslam,

Came across your post on the MIT GenAI Divide research a few months back. The point about 95% of enterprises stuck in high-adoption but low-transformation mode resonated. It matches what I keep running into: teams picking up LangGraph but without the governance layer that makes those pipelines reliable past a demo.

For context: I rebuilt a RAG chatbot at Vivansh into a fully agentic LangGraph system with tool-calling over 10+ platform APIs, 2-stage permission-aware retrieval (250+ permissions per query, no caching because permission profiles change too fast), 80% MRR on 1,000+ queries/day for a 500-user EHS platform. The part that took longest was the state machine design, not the LLM integration. That architecture-first gap you described is real.

Tredence's agentic analytics work is something I've been tracking. If your team is hiring on the ML side, would a 15-min call make sense?

Job Description
Senior AI/ML Engineer - Agentic AI Systems

Tredence is building an agentic analytics platform capable of root cause analysis, hypothesis testing, question expansion, and automated report generation.

Key Responsibilities:
1. Agentic Analytics Pipelines: Designing automated workflows that can dynamically generate and refine hypotheses to understand the 'why' behind data fluctuations.
2. Event-Driven Execution: Building systems that trigger actions, diagnoses, or alerts automatically in response to specific data anomalies or pipeline events.
3. Automated Root Cause Analysis (RCA): Utilizing multi-agent systems to sift through complex datasets, trace errors or performance drops to their origin, and provide actionable fixes.
4. Hypothesis Testing: Engineering logic that allows AI agents to formulate multiple scenarios, test them against data distributions, and validate assumptions.
5. Question Expansion and Reporting: Expanding basic user inquiries into deeper exploratory questions, automatically analyzing the results, and generating comprehensive human-readable reports.

Expected Qualifications and Tech Stack:
- LLMs and Prompt Engineering: Strong proficiency in working with advanced LLMs
- Engineering and Integration: Hands-on experience with modern tech stacks including Python, LangChain, LlamaIndex, vector databases, and MLOps practices
- Context Engineering
- Stack: Python, LangGraph, LangChain, FastAPI, Celery, Redis, PostgreSQL, Databricks, Docker, Kubernetes, Azure

Research Summary

TrueFoundry is Series A-backed ($19M, Peak XV + Intel Capital) Bengaluru-based AI infra company building enterprise agentic AI platform: AI Gateway, AI Deploy, MCP Gateway. 40+ employees; team tripled in 2025. Recognized in Gartner AI Gateway Market Guide (Feb 2026). Clients: NVIDIA, Siemens, ResMed, Zscaler. JD wants 4-10yr engineers with production LangGraph/LangChain, RAG, vector DB, and observability experience. Amit's 2-stage Milvus RAG + LangGraph agentic upgrade at Vivansh is a direct match. Email pattern: firstname@truefoundry.com (confirmed via parth@truefoundry.com). 7 contacts found: 3 Tier 1, 3 Tier 3, 1 Tier 4.

Tailoring Notes

1. Summary: rewrote to lead with 'agentic AI systems' and call out LangChain/LangGraph by name (JD explicitly requires agent orchestration framework experience); added 'production LLM integration across FastAPI services' to match their infra focus. 2. Technical Skills reordered: 'LLMs and Agentic AI' moved to top (was 3rd) -- renamed to foreground LangChain, LangGraph, VLLM, which are TrueFoundry's core stack. 'Vector DBs and Retrieval' kept 2nd (Milvus is central to their product). 'Languages and Frameworks' dropped to 3rd with FastAPI and Pydantic leading. Hardware/Edge ML moved to bottom -- JD is pure software/LLM infra. 3. Work experience bullets unchanged in content; LangChain/LangGraph mention in Vivansh bullet already prominent. IIT Delhi bullet kept -- the 1,000+ model config benchmark signals rigorous evaluation methodology matching their observability/benchmarking JD requirement. 4. Projects: Voicer and WeepScope kept as single compact bullets -- they demonstrate national recognition and breadth without taking space from the ML platform story.

Subject: production LangGraph + Milvus -- relevant to TrueFoundry?
Hi Chirag,

Saw your ICLR 2025 LLM paper roundup on LinkedIn a few months back. The bit about attention and retrieval efficiency lined up with a problem I was grinding through at the same time -- we had an agentic chatbot on LangGraph hitting 8-15k tokens per request and I needed to know which retrieval patterns were actually worth the cost at that token budget.

I'm 2 years into production AI at Vivansh Infotech (before that, Silver Touch). The system I spent the most time on: a LangGraph state machine orchestrating 2-stage Milvus retrieval with 250+ live permission checks per query, serving 500+ concurrent users. Before that at Silver Touch, I led the migration from in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API and benchmarked Milvus vs Qdrant vs FAISS on 50k+ query/month production data -- Milvus won at 95% MRR vs FAISS's 45%.

If TrueFoundry is hiring on the ML platform side this quarter, I'd be glad to hear how you think about the retrieval + agent orchestration problems you're working through. Open to a 15-min call next Tuesday or Thursday.

Anant Agarwal Principal Applied Scientist, GenAI
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: anant@truefoundry.com
Co-launched The Gen Academy in 2025 with Arvind Narayanamurthy -- trained ~2,000 professionals in RAG architectures, agentic systems, MCP, context engineering, and LangChain/LangGraph. Also published an IEEE 2025 paper on automated prompt optimization using LLM-powered Genetic Algorithms. IIT Kharagpur + ISB.
Initial Email
Subject: RAG + agentic systems -- worth a quick chat?
Hi Anant,

Came across the Gen Academy work you and Arvind put together -- training 2,000 engineers on RAG, MCP, and context engineering in six months is a real signal about where the market is moving. The context engineering workshop in particular stood out; I've been working through exactly that problem in production, figuring out how much context is too much before retrieval quality starts hurting response accuracy.

Quick context on my end: at Vivansh I built a 2-stage LangGraph orchestration system where the first stage handles 250+ permission checks per query before any retrieval happens, and the second stage hits a Milvus index with 10,000+ chunks from 750+ courses. Response time holds at ~8s under 500+ concurrent users. Before that at Silver Touch, I ran a head-to-head benchmark of Milvus, Qdrant, and FAISS on 50k+ monthly production queries -- the 95% vs 45% MRR gap between Milvus and FAISS is what convinced us to migrate.

If TrueFoundry has room on the ML side, I'd love 15 minutes to hear how you're thinking about the applied GenAI problems your team is working through. Tuesday or Thursday next week works for me.

Amit

Aditi Gupta Senior Machine Learning Engineer
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: aditi@truefoundry.com
Joined TrueFoundry Sept 2024; builds and deploys AI chat agents; integrates LLM eval frameworks into the platform. Former Lead Data Scientist at Delhivery. Her work wiring LLM evaluation into a production platform is the same class of problem Amit faced benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data before selecting BGE-M3 + reranker.
Initial Email
Subject: LLM eval frameworks in production -- quick question
Hi Aditi,

Was reading about the LLM eval work you've been wiring into TrueFoundry's platform. That problem of building evaluation that actually reflects production behavior -- not just benchmark scores -- is one I've been dealing with directly.

At Silver Touch I ran a head-to-head on 6 embedding models (BGE small/base/large, Stella, SPLADE, BGE-M3) using our live query traffic rather than any standard benchmark. MRR on production data was the only honest signal. BGE-M3 plus a reranker won, and the entire migration from FAISS to Milvus improved MRR from 45% to 95%. At Vivansh the same evaluation-first mindset shaped how I built a 2-stage LangGraph retrieval system: measure permission-filtering precision, measure retrieval recall, measure end-to-end latency under 500+ concurrent users. Iterating without those numbers is guessing.

If your team has room for someone who approaches AI engineering this way, I'd be glad to hear what you're building. Worth a 15-min call?

Amit

Kashish Kumar Senior Machine Learning Engineer
IC queued LinkedIn
Email: kashish@truefoundry.com
Has a personal site (kashishkumar.com) and Medium blog documenting his ML work; former ExxonMobil. His public writing habit signals he values sharing technical depth -- same way Amit's benchmark data (FAISS 45% vs Milvus 95% MRR) tells the story better than any credential.
Initial Email
Subject: ML engineer with production RAG + LangGraph -- worth connecting?
Hi Kashish,

Found your personal site while going through TrueFoundry's team -- the Medium writing habit is a good signal, it usually means someone is actually processing what they're building rather than just shipping and moving on.

I've been doing the same kind of documentation in my own work. The most useful piece I put together was a breakdown of a production vector DB evaluation: FAISS at 45% MRR, Qdrant at 85%, Milvus at 95%, all on 50k+ live queries/month at Silver Touch. That benchmark drove the Milvus migration. At Vivansh after that, I built a LangGraph agentic RAG system that enforces 250+ user permissions per query against a 10,000+ chunk Milvus index. Both are in production; neither is a demo.

If TrueFoundry has room for that kind of engineer, you'd be the right person to connect me with whoever is hiring on the ML side. Worth a quick chat?

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

Subject: AI/ML engineer -- production RAG, LangGraph, Milvus
Hi Parth,

Reaching out directly because TrueFoundry's ML platform stack matches what I've been building in production.

Short version: 2+ years shipping production AI systems. At Vivansh I built a LangGraph-orchestrated agentic RAG chatbot -- 2-stage Milvus retrieval, 250+ live permission checks per query, serving 500+ concurrent users at 80% MRR. Before that at Silver Touch, I rebuilt the RAG infrastructure for a 50k+ query/month platform: migrated from FAISS to Milvus (45% to 95% MRR), moved in-code Llama-2-7b to a VLLM-served API, cut latency from 16s to 8s. Also shipped an AI form builder (GPT-4o + Qwen3-Coder + GPT-4o-mini pipeline, 200+ forms generated in month 1) and a video course generation pipeline (10,000+ source clips, AWS Lambda rendering, 1 week to 10 minutes).

Azure AI Engineer Associate certified. Full profile and projects: amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai

If there's an open ML or AI platform role I should be looking at, I'd appreciate a pointer. If not, happy to be on your radar.

Thanks,
Amit

Job Description
Senior AI/ML Engineer: LLM & Agent Stack at TrueFoundry (Bengaluru, India)

About TrueFoundry:
TrueFoundry builds Enterprise-Ready Agentic AI infrastructure -- AI Gateway, AI Deploy, MCP Gateway, and Skills Registry. Processes 10B+ requests/month for clients including ResMed, Siemens Healthineers, NVIDIA, and Zscaler. Series A ($19M) backed by Sequoia/Peak XV and Intel Capital.

Role Summary:
Design and own core components that enable enterprise customers to run production agentic AI safely and efficiently, including building robust orchestration for multi-step agents (graph/stateful workflows), model/routing logic, observability and policy enforcement (cost, data residency, rate limiting), and integrating upstream tooling like LangGraph, LangChain, vector stores, and specialized LLM runtimes.

Key Responsibilities:
- Architect and implement scalable agent orchestration patterns (graph-based executors, state management, multi-agent coordination) for production workloads
- Own critical integrations: model adapters, LLM gateway hooks, vector DBs, tools & external APIs, and the platform's LLMops flows
- Build and improve tracing, benchmarking and observability for LLMs and agents, token/cost accounting, latency p95, throughput, and correctness checks
- Drive design for guardrails including moderation hooks, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and audit trails
- Mentor junior engineers, conduct design reviews, and enhance engineering practices
- Work directly with strategic customers prototyping complex agentic solutions

Must-Have Requirements:
- 4-10 years of software engineering with distributed systems, infrastructure, or ML platform experience
- Deep practical experience deploying LLMs in production (RAG, retrieval, embeddings)
- Hands-on experience with agent orchestration frameworks (LangGraph / LangChain or custom agent runtimes)
- Proven track record building observability, cost controls, and policy enforcement

Preferred Qualifications:
- Open-source contributions to LLM orchestration tools
- Enterprise deployment experience (on-prem/cloud hybrid, data residency, compliance)
- Security, privacy, or model governance background
- Cross-functional project leadership and customer engagement

Location: Bengaluru, India
Salary: 35-55 LPA (from job board listings)

Salary30-45 LPA
Hiringactively hiring

Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Internal teams use the exact stack: LangGraph, RAG, Milvus, FastAPI, Azure SQL for customer-support chatbot. Active SE III postings with GenAI/LLM/Agentic AI requirements.

Notes

Stack match is unusually exact (Milvus + FastAPI + RAG + LangGraph in production). Bengaluru-only role.

Research Summary

Walmart Global Tech India (10K+ employees, Bengaluru) is hiring SE-III GenAI engineers to build agentic AI and RAG systems. CTO Suresh Kumar published 'All in on Agents' (Jul 2025) announcing 4 super agents. The tech stack -- LangGraph, Milvus, FastAPI, RAG -- is an exact match with Amit's production work. Internal engineers like Urvashi Ramdasani and Jigyasa Kodnani are already building LangGraph + RAG systems at Walmart. Email pattern: firstname.lastname@walmart.com. 9 contacts across Tier 1 (senior ML), Tier 3 (mid ML), and Tier 4 (recruiters).

Tailoring Notes

1. Summary rewritten to lead with 'agentic AI and RAG systems' and call out LangGraph, Milvus, 50K queries/month, 95% MRR, and FastAPI explicitly -- these are the three things Walmart's JD emphasizes above all else. Dropped 'edge ML' reference (not relevant to this GenAI SE-III role). 2. Technical Skills renamed first category to 'Agentic AI and Frameworks' and reordered: LangGraph and LangChain lead (Walmart's explicit orchestration requirement), FastAPI next (backend infra match), then Python and ML libraries. 3. LLMs category now leads with 'Agentic AI, RAG, Hybrid Retrieval' to match JD emphasis on retrieval architecture. 4. Infrastructure: added Azure explicitly (Walmart is Azure-aligned), added LangSmith (Walmart blog mentions it for agentic observability), removed Hardware/IoT tools not relevant to this role. 5. Dropped WeepScope project (pure hardware TinyML -- not relevant to GenAI SE-III). Kept Voicer (Samsung Top 10 among 70K+ is a strong proof point for technical credibility). 6. Hardware/IoT skills bullet removed entirely -- Walmart role is pure software/AI.

Contacts & Emails 9 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 9 queued
Prakhar Patidar Staff Machine Learning Engineer
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: prakhar.patidar@walmart.com
Staff MLE at Walmart during the 'All in on Agents' push (CTO Suresh Kumar, Jul 2025) -- likely building or scaling the ML infra behind Walmart's 4 super agents. The 'Why solving Agent and RAG Sprawl is essential' blog (Apr 2025) shows the exact production challenge he is working on.
Initial Email
Subject: Walmart's RAG sprawl post -- built something similar
Hi Prakhar,

Came across Walmart Global Tech's "Why solving Agent and RAG Sprawl is essential" post a few weeks ago. The core problem -- managing many agents and RAG pipelines without them drifting or stepping on each other -- is something I have been working through for the past year.

At Vivansh I built a permission-aware RAG chatbot on LangGraph where 250+ per-user access controls had to be enforced in real time across 10,000+ chunks. Migrated from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking both on production data, and got MRR from 45% to 95% on 50K+ queries/month. The hardest part was not the retrieval -- it was keeping agent state coherent across tool calls when permissions varied per query.

If there is a role on your team for someone with this kind of production background, I would appreciate 15 minutes. Open to a call next Tuesday or Thursday.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | github.com/amit-gohel

Subject: re: Walmart's RAG sprawl post -- built something similar

Bumping this in case it got buried. Since I sent the last note, I put together a short writeup on the permission index approach -- a separate Milvus collection used only for access control, cross-referenced before every retrieval call. Happy to share it if useful.

Amit

Initial Email
Subject: question about Walmart's applied AI stack
Hi Vishal,

Read Suresh Kumar's "All in on Agents" post when it came out -- the part about 4 unified super agents replacing fragmented AI tools was interesting. As someone building production agentic systems, I am curious how the Applied AI team approaches the orchestration layer, particularly for the associate and developer agents.

Quick context: at Vivansh I built a LangGraph agentic chatbot with a stateful memory layer in PostgreSQL and 2-stage Milvus retrieval enforcing 250+ per-user permissions in real time. FastAPI with 4 async workers, 1,000+ queries/day. Session management across tool calls was the hard part -- keeping agent context coherent when permissions changed mid-session.

If your team is hiring on the applied AI side, I would love 15 minutes to hear how you think about agent state management at Walmart's scale. Open to next Tuesday or Thursday.

Amit

Initial Email
Subject: your LangGraph orchestrator -- ran into the same thing
Hi Urvashi,

Saw your LinkedIn mention the autonomous agentic orchestrator you built on FastAPI + LangGraph with stateful memory in PostgreSQL. That is basically the same architecture I shipped at Vivansh -- LangGraph for agent state, FastAPI with 4 async workers, PostgreSQL for session memory, and Milvus for retrieval.

The hard part for me was managing tool call state across permission-gated queries -- 250+ per-user access controls checked before every retrieval step, 1,000+ queries/day. I imagine you ran into similar edge cases with async tool execution at scale.

If there is room on the team for someone with this background, I would appreciate an intro to whoever is hiring on your side. Happy to chat briefly if that is easier.

Job Description
Software Engineer III -- ML Engineer (GenAI) | Walmart Global Tech India | Bengaluru

Core requirements (synthesized from LinkedIn, Instahyre, and public job data):

- Agentic AI and LangGraph: Build multi-agent systems with conditional routing, state management, reflection loops, and tool-calling for reasoning tasks
- Enterprise RAG: Design production-grade RAG pipelines -- custom chunking strategies, semantic search, hybrid retrieval (keyword + vector search), embedding model selection and evaluation (sentence transformers)
- Vector Databases (Milvus): High-scale vector store expertise -- indexing strategies, query scalability, integration with 1M+ enterprise records
- Engineering and MLOps: Production Python, Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD pipelines, LangSmith observability for agentic workflow tracing
- Core ML: B.S./M.S. in CS or Engineering, ML algorithms, data structures, LLM fine-tuning (PEFT/LoRA)
- 3+ years software engineering with exposure to ranking, recommendation, or ML systems at scale
- FastAPI or equivalent backend experience
- Azure cloud services

Context: Walmart has deployed 4 AI super agents (Sparky for customers, associate, supplier, developer). Internal teams are solving 'Agent and RAG Sprawl' at enterprise scale. The Bengaluru IDC is a core engineering hub.

Whatfix

Bengaluru · AI Engineer (Backend + AI) — 2 to 5 yrs

Notes

Bengaluru HQ; JD nearly mirrors Amit's resume; 5-day in-office policy.

Research Summary

Whatfix is a $270M-funded DAP platform (Series E) racing toward IPO with AI products targeted at 20% of revenue by 2026. Their flagship AI work: ScreenSense (context+intent AI engine), Seek (enterprise AI agent -- first to complete Salesforce Trailhead challenges), and a new 0-to-1 Enterprise Search product. The Enterprise Search role requires RAG, vector search (BERT/Sentence Transformers), Elasticsearch, and LLM integration -- a direct match to Amit's Milvus/FAISS/LangChain stack. Email pattern: first.last@whatfix.com (88.2%). 9 contacts identified across senior AI/ML, junior ML, and TA roles.

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to lead with information retrieval and NLP: 'RAG pipelines serving 50,000+ queries/month', 'BGE-M3, Sentence Transformers, SPLADE', 'semantic search and scalable backend AI systems' -- mirrors JD phrases 'RAG pipelines', 'vector search', 'neural ranking models', 'scalable systems'. Skills section reordered: Vector DBs and Retrieval moved to top (JD leads with vector search + embeddings), LLMs/NLP second, Languages third, Infrastructure fourth. Hardware/IoT line removed (JD is pure search/AI/backend). Sentence Transformers explicitly named in skills to match JD must-have. WeepScope project removed (hardware-only, no signal for this role). Voicer kept for Samsung Solve for Tomorrow credential. IIT Delhi internship retained -- 1,000+ model evaluations bullet shows rigor relevant to embedding model evaluation the JD implies.

At Vivansh right now, building a permission-aware agentic system with LangGraph.

Subject: Whatfix Seek + the retrieval layer
Hi Kanchan,

Seek completing the Trailhead Admin Challenges in October was a meaningful milestone. An AI agent that can navigate and complete certification workflows end-to-end means the retrieval and context layer is working at a level most agents don't reach.

I've been building that retrieval layer for the past two years. At Silver Touch: 50K+ queries/month, a full migration from FAISS to Milvus, 6 embedding models evaluated on production data, latency cut from 16s to 8s via intent classification upfront. At Vivansh now: a 2-stage permission-aware system with 250+ granular access filters per query using LangGraph.

The Enterprise Search opening caught my eye. 0-to-1 with RAG and vector search is the exact kind of build I want to be part of. If you're filling that role and a 15-minute conversation would help, I'm around.

Subject: Enterprise Search architecture at Whatfix
Hi Gaurav,

At Silver Touch, I built retrieval infrastructure for a multi-tenant chatbot at 50K+ queries/month. That meant choosing between vector DBs (FAISS vs Qdrant vs Milvus), running embedding benchmarks across 6 candidates on production corpora, and designing a 2-stage retrieval setup that got MRR to 95%. Not Elasticsearch-native, but the same indexing and ranking tradeoffs apply.

Figured it's worth asking: is the team actively interviewing for that role, and what does the technical bar look like for E5? If not the right time, no worries.

Subject: NLP at Whatfix - quick question

Your TUM ML background plus NLP work at Whatfix caught my attention. I'm an ML engineer who's been working on semantic retrieval and NLP pipelines -- document parsing, embedding evaluation, query preprocessing.

At Silver Touch, I ran a benchmark of 6 embedding models (BGE-M3, BGE small/base/large, SPLADE, Stella) on a production document corpus, then built a custom retrieval setup with BGE-M3 plus a cross-encoder reranker. A lot of the interesting work was figuring out where sparse retrieval (SPLADE) outperformed dense retrieval and where it didn't.

I'm looking at the Enterprise Search opening at Whatfix. Is it a team you know well, and would you say the NLP/ML component of the role is a significant part of the day-to-day work?

Job Description
Software Engineer E5 - Enterprise Search

Bengaluru, Karnataka, India | Engineering | Full-time

Who are we?

Whatfix is an AI platform advancing the 'userization' of enterprise applications, empowering companies to maximize the ROI of their digital investments. Technology needs adoption. It's no different for AI. As AI reshapes roles, workflows, and human-machine interactions, it also introduces new layers of complexity and user friction. This is where Whatfix plays a pivotal role. A decade old DNA of empowering people to succeed with technology and not replacing them. We call this philosophy Userization: the belief that technology must adapt to the user, not the other way around.

At the heart of userization philosophy is ScreenSense, our proprietary AI engine, which continuously interprets both the context of what users are doing in an application or an AI tool and the intent behind their actions. By combining these signals, Whatfix delivers real-time guidance, nudges, knowledge, and automation directly in the flow of work.

This intelligence powers our entire product suite.

Digital Adoption helps users get productive faster.
Product Analytics uncovers friction and closes adoption gaps.
Mirror allows employees to train in safe, simulated environments.

These are embedded with Whatfix AI Agents which supercharge creation, insights, and user guidance.

Our upcoming AI-first products are already creating a buzz in the market.

Seek is an AI-native assistant that not only knows your business context but can also act across applications to get work done on your behalf.
Whatfix Mirror 2.0 is the world's only System plus Role simulation with a complete assessment to lead the Gen AI simulation category.

About the Role

We are looking for a passionate and experienced Software Engineer (E5 / E6 level) to join our Enterprise Search team, which is at the core of redefining how users discover and interact with information across Whatfix's digital adoption platform. This is a unique opportunity to solve deep information retrieval and search relevance challenges using scalable infrastructure, cutting-edge NLP, and Generative AI.

As an engineer at this level, you'll be expected to operate with strong ownership, lead cross-team technical initiatives, and influence design choices that directly impact user experience and business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Build a 0-to-1 Enterprise Search product with a strong focus on scalability, performance, and relevance.
Lead proof-of-concept efforts to validate ideas quickly and align with business goals.
Architect and implement robust, maintainable, and scalable systems for indexing, querying, and ranking.
Develop data pipelines, implement automation for reliability, and ensure strong observability and monitoring.
Work closely with Product Managers and Designers to translate user needs into data-driven, intuitive search experiences.
Guide and support junior engineers through code reviews, technical direction, and best practices.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams (data, platform, infra) to deliver cohesive and high-impact solutions.

What We're Looking For

Must-Have Skills:

Familiarity with LLMs, RAG pipelines, or knowledge graph integrations.
Deep expertise in information retrieval, search engines (Lucene, Elasticsearch, Solr).
Experience with vector search, embeddings, and/or neural ranking models (e.g., BERT, Sentence Transformers).
Strong programming skills in Java, Python, or Go.
Familiarity with scalable data processing frameworks (e.g., Spark, Kafka, Flink).
Good understanding of system design, APIs, caching, and performance tuning.

Nice-to-Have:

Experience with enterprise content connectors (SharePoint, Confluence, Jira, etc.).
Experience working in a SaaS, B2B, or product-first environment.

Qualifications

6-10+ years of experience building backend systems, infrastructure, or AI platforms at scale.
Proven ability to own and deliver complex features independently, collaborate across teams, and mentor peers in a fast-paced environment.
Demonstrated experience leading initiatives with significant technical and organizational impact.

Notes

Eternal group also includes Blinkit. NLP work on reviews, photos, restaurant search. Python heavy. Weekday: 13.4 LPA @1yr, 21.7 LPA @5yr.

Research Summary

Zomato (Eternal) is a 5000+ employee public company running ML at serious scale: search ranking, recommendation engines, LLM-powered customer support (Nugget, 15M+ conversations/month), and NLP for reviews/spam/dish tagging. Expanded OpenAI partnership in Feb 2026. GenAI team grew from 3 to 20+ engineers. Their ML Runtime (Feature Store, Model Store, Model Serving API Gateway) and multi-model approach closely mirrors Amit's production work. Key hook: Amit's FAISS->Milvus migration and BGE-M3 benchmarking is the exact kind of data-driven infra decision their team makes. Agentic RAG with LangGraph also maps to Nugget's architecture.

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to lead with NLP + retrieval systems and LLM optimization -- mirrors Zomato's core ML mandate (search ranking, recommendation, LLM fine-tuning). Added '1,000+ queries/day' and 'LLM optimization, search quality, scalable ML serving' to summary to match their JD language. Skills reordered: LLMs/NLP first (since NLP + LLMs are Zomato's primary concern), Vector DBs second (search/retrieval infra), Languages third, Infrastructure fourth. Hardware/IoT skill line dropped entirely -- irrelevant for this role. Added Fine-tuning, Text Classification, Sentiment Analysis to LLMs/NLP skill line. Added 'multi-index architectures, reranking, adaptive chunking' to Vector DBs line. Projects: Reordered so WeepScope leads (audio classification, research paper, empirical MFE vs MFCC comparison -- shows NLP-adjacent ML rigor); expanded WeepScope bullet to highlight empirical feature comparison. Voicer rewrote to focus on model training + dataset (94.1% accuracy, custom 200 samples/class dataset) rather than hardware. Work experience bullets unchanged -- all highly relevant as-is.

Hi Naresh,

Bumping this in case it got buried. One thing I should have mentioned: after the FAISS to Milvus swap at Silver Touch, the bottleneck shifted from retrieval accuracy to query preprocessing latency. Same kind of query preprocessing your scalable ML post touches on with the Feature Compute Engine.

Vivek Gupta Senior Developer, Nugget
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: vivek.gupta@zomato.com
Building Nugget, Zomato's AI-native CX platform that now handles 15M+ conversations/month at 85% resolution. Nugget uses Llama 70B for intent detection + Llama 8B for chat, MongoDB Atlas, policy-layer guardrails, multi-class classification. Launched as enterprise product in 2025. MongoDB case study quote: 'conversations are fragmented, context is constantly evolving, and automation must coexist with human judgment.'
Initial Email
Subject: Nugget's fragmented context problem and LangGraph
Hi Vivek,

Read the MongoDB case study on Nugget -- your line about conversations being fragmented and context constantly evolving is the exact problem I ran into building a permission-aware RAG chatbot at Vivansh. Our solution was a LangGraph state machine: permission filtering node using a Milvus permission index cross-referenced against PostgreSQL, then content retrieval from 10,000+ chunks, then GPT-4o for answer generation. 500+ concurrent users, 1,000+ queries/day, 250+ permissions enforced per query with no caching possible because two users asking the same question may need different answers.

The parallel to Nugget's intent classification and policy guardrail layer is pretty direct. We also ran Llama on-prem via VLLM for a different client project, so the multi-model routing approach is familiar.

If there's an ML or backend AI role on the Nugget side or the broader Zomato org, I'd like 15 minutes to hear what problems you're currently working on.

Amit
github.com/amit-gohel | amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai | amitgohel.dev/chat
Follow-up 1 sched 2026-05-20
Subject: re: Nugget's fragmented context problem and LangGraph
Hi Vivek,

Following up. One thing I didn't mention: we also built a Selenium crawler that recognized dynamic UI elements (tabs, dropdowns, accordions) to ingest hidden content into our RAG index. Total chunks ended up at 10,000+ across website pages, knowledge base, and course content, with metadata-based filtering at query time. Useful for keeping the index clean without rebuilding constantly -- possibly relevant if Nugget's enterprise expansion means more diverse data sources.

That data-driven framing is the same mindset I used when selecting models for a form generation pipeline: GPT-4o produced reasonable blueprints but Qwen3-Coder-480B beat it on structural accuracy after testing both across 50+ form templates. Empirical selection over just using the biggest available model.

I'm 2 years into production ML: RAG at 50K+ queries/month, agentic LangGraph pipelines at Vivansh, on-prem vLLM serving at Silver Touch. Following what Zomato's team is shipping on the LLM optimization side. If your team is hiring or you know who is, I'd appreciate a pointer.

Job Description
Sr. AI Engineer / Machine Learning Engineer -- Zomato (Eternal)
Location: Gurgaon / Bangalore | Experience: 3-7 years | Salary: 20-34 LPA

About the role:
As an ML Engineer at Zomato, you will be responsible for designing cutting-edge scalable products to meet Zomato's rapidly growing business needs. You will take end-to-end ownership of Machine Learning systems -- from data pipelines and training to real-time prediction engines.

Key responsibilities:
- Maintain and optimize large-scale ML infrastructure that powers Search ranking models
- Build and improve restaurant and dish recommendation engines
- Work with engineers to implement algorithms and systems with strong positive impact on user-facing products
- NLP applications: understanding reviews/comments to extract dish tags, review summaries, spam signals, sentiment analysis
- LLM optimization: fine-tuning, RLHF, scaling AI solutions for food and grocery experiences
- Own end-to-end ML systems: feature engineering, model training, real-time serving
- Work with Kafka, Airflow, Spark, Pulsar for data pipelines
- Contribute to ML Runtime: Feature Compute Engine, Feature Store, Model Store, Model Serving API Gateway

Required skills:
- Strong command of Python (primary) or Go as backend language
- Solid engineering principles, data structures, and algorithms
- Experience with large-scale ML in production
- Familiarity with generative AI, LLM fine-tuning, RLHF, stable diffusion
- NLP expertise: text classification, embeddings, retrieval
- Real-time prediction systems at scale
- Vector databases and embedding models

Nice to have:
- Experience with Kafka/Airflow/Spark/Pulsar
- LangChain / LangGraph / agentic AI systems
- On-premise model hosting (vLLM or similar)
- Multi-model orchestration architectures

About Zomato ML:
Zomato uses ML across: search ranking (neural network with Word2Vec, entity recognition), dish tagging, review mining, photo classification, personalized recommendations, spam detection, delivery logistics optimization, and generative AI (Nugget CX platform, ZIA restaurant assistant). The team runs ML Runtime consisting of Feature Compute Engine, Feature Store, Model Store, and Model Serving API Gateway.

ZS Associates

Bengaluru · AI Engineer - Machine Learning (Max.AI team, 1-3 yrs)
done
LocationBengaluru
RoleAI Engineer - Machine Learning (Max.AI team, 1-3 yrs)
Salary18-28 LPA
FundingPrivate (management consulting firm)
Hiringactively hiring
Priority93
Source CSVcompany_data_1.csv
Careers pageJob URLLinkedIn
Match Reason

Dedicated Max.AI engineering unit building GenAI products; Job ID 32712 Bengaluru 1-3 yrs ML; Python + MLOps (MLflow) + production ML pipelines; PySpark; Airflow; SageMaker/Kubeflow; AWS/Azure/GCP

Outreach Angle

ZS Atlas Intelligence patent (May 2026) + Max.AI Platforms and Products unit; Anirban Das team's AI Factory for Agentic Solutions paper at PMSA 2026; Amit's RAG/MLOps/PySpark background maps directly to JD

Notes

127 ZS Associates jobs on Naukri; large GenAI practice across pharma/healthcare; email pattern first.last@zs.com (85%)

Research Summary

ZS (formerly ZS Associates) is a $2.7B management consulting + technology firm for life sciences/pharma/healthcare with 10K+ employees. Their Max.AI / Platforms and Products team (Req ID 32712, Bengaluru) builds ML engineering platforms using Python, PySpark, MLflow, Airflow, SageMaker, Kubeflow, and cloud. ZS just received a U.S. patent for ZS Atlas Intelligence (May 2026). Their Tech Innovation Lab published an AI Factory for Enterprise Agentic Solutions paper at PMSA 2026. Email pattern: first.last@zs.com. 8 contacts captured: 3 Tier 1 (senior ML), 3 Tier 3 (junior/mid ML), 2 Tier 4 (recruiters).

Tailoring Notes

Summary rewritten to lead with 'ML engineering platforms' and 'inferencing pipelines' mirroring ZS JD language; quantified MRR improvement (45% to 95%) frames MLOps track record without using the word 'RAG'. Skills reordered: Languages/Frameworks first (Python/SQL prominent per JD), then new 'ML Engineering and MLOps' category (feature engineering, inferencing pipelines, model performance tracking, VLLM), then Cloud/AWS (explicit JD requirement), then LLMs/AI, then Vector DBs -- Hardware/IoT row removed entirely. IIT Delhi reduced to 1 bullet emphasizing the 1,000+ model configuration evaluation (maps to 'ML fundamentals' and 'evaluate latest architecture patterns' in JD). Projects: Voicer swapped for On-Premise OCR + Document Entity Extraction pipeline to show production data pipeline depth (JD calls out inferencing pipelines and feature engineering); WeepScope kept as 2nd project to show ML model development fundamentals and research depth (CNN+GRU, co-authored paper). Certifications kept as-is (Azure cert is JD-adjacent).

Contacts & Emails 8 contacts · 0 sent · 0 replied · 0 approved · 8 queued
Anirban Das Senior AI Engineer, Tech Innovation Lab | LLMOps | Gen AI | NLP
EM/Lead queued LinkedIn
Email: anirban.das@zs.com
His team published 'AI Factory Approach for Enterprise Agentic Solutions' paper accepted at PMSA 2026 Annual conference (1 month ago). He works in ZS's Tech Innovation Lab building LLMOps/GenAI systems for pharma clients.
Initial Email
Subject: AI Factory paper at PMSA 2026
Hi Anirban,

Saw the PMSA 2026 acceptance for the AI Factory approach your team built. Getting enterprise agentic work into a pharma conference is a different kind of validation.

Quick context on why I'm writing: I'm 2 years into production ML at Vivansh Infotech. Built permission-aware agentic RAG (LangChain/LangGraph) serving 500+ concurrent enterprise users with 250+ permissions enforced per query, and multi-model inference pipelines with GPT-4o, Qwen3-Coder, and Gemini. Before that, Silver Touch: rebuilt a 50K-queries/month retrieval stack from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models; MRR from 45% to 95%.

I came across the AI Engineer (ML) role at ZS (Req ID 32712) and the fit looks real. Python, MLOps, inferencing pipelines, production-ready code at scale.

If you have 15 minutes, I'd be curious how the Tech Innovation Lab approaches model drift and feedback loops at ZS's client scale. That context would help me decide whether to apply formally.

Amit

Most of my production work has been one-off systems that turn into informal platforms. The deliberate architecture piece is what I want to learn more about.

If the timing doesn't work, totally fine. Happy to connect when you're next looking at ML hires.

Viresh Dhawan Manager, Data Science & AI Products

Initial Email
Subject: Decision intelligence at 40+ clients, question
Hi Viresh,

Your framing around decision intelligence at ZS caught my attention. Building that across 40+ pharma clients means standardizing ML decision layers for very different data environments. That's a harder problem than most ML infra work.

Background: two years on production ML pipelines. Rebuilt a 50K-queries/month retrieval system at Silver Touch (from FAISS to Milvus after benchmarking 6 embedding models on production data, MRR from 45% to 95%). Now building multi-model agentic AI at Vivansh handling 500+ concurrent enterprise users. The MLOps side -- model performance tracking, latency optimization, feedback loops -- is where most of the interesting problems lived.

ZS's AI Engineer (ML) role in Bengaluru (Req ID 32712) is what I'm looking at. The fit on Python, MLOps, and inferencing pipelines looks clean.

If you're open to it, I'd be curious what the most common ML deployment failure mode looks like across ZS's client portfolio. Even 10 minutes would be useful.

Amit
amitgohel.dev | linkedin.com/in/amitgohel-ai
Follow-up 1
Subject: Re: Decision intelligence at 40+ clients, question
Hi Viresh,

Following up on this in case my first note got buried.

One thing I've been thinking about since: the challenge of decision intelligence at pharma scale is that the ground truth for "correct" recommendations changes as regulatory context changes. That's a model drift problem with external triggers, not just data drift. I don't know how ZS handles it but I'd guess it requires fairly opinionated MLOps pipelines.

If now's not a good time, no problem. Happy to reconnect when things open up on the ML side.

Amit
amitgohel.dev
Follow-up 2

Job Description
AI Engineer - Machine Learning | Req ID: 32712 | Location: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India | Category: Platforms and Products

ZS is a place where passion changes lives. As a management consulting and technology firm focused on improving life and how we live it, we transform ideas into impact by bringing together data, science, technology and human ingenuity to deliver better outcomes for all. Here you'll work side-by-side with a powerful collective of thinkers and experts shaping life-changing solutions for patients, caregivers and consumers, worldwide. ZSers drive impact by bringing a client-first mentality to each and every engagement. We partner collaboratively with our clients to develop custom solutions and technology products that create value and deliver company results across critical areas of their business. Bring your curiosity for learning, bold ideas, courage and passion to drive life-changing impact to ZS.

What You'll Do:
Build, Refine and Use ML Engineering platforms and components.
Scaling machine learning algorithms to work on massive data sets and strict SLAs.
Build and orchestrate model pipelines including feature engineering, inferencing and continuous model training.
Implement ML Ops including model KPI measurements, tracking, model drift & model feedback loop.
Collaborate with client facing teams to understand business context at a high level and contribute in technical requirement gathering.
Implement basic features aligning with technical requirements.
Write production-ready code that is easily testable, understood by other developers and accounts for edge cases and errors.
Ensure highest quality of deliverables by following architecture/design guidelines, coding best practices, periodic design/code reviews.
Write unit tests as well as higher level tests to handle expected edge cases and errors gracefully, as well as happy paths.
Uses bug tracking, code review, version control and other tools to organize and deliver work.
Participate in scrum calls and agile ceremonies, and effectively communicate work progress, issues and dependencies.
Consistently contribute in researching & evaluating latest architecture patterns/technologies through rapid learning, conducting proof-of-concepts and creating prototype solutions.

What You'll Bring:
A master's or bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field from a top university.
1-3 years' hands-on experience in ML development.
Good understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning.
Strong programming expertise in Python, PySpark/Scala.
Expertise in crafting ML Models for high performance and scalability.
Experience in implementing feature engineering, inferencing pipelines, and real time model predictions.
Experience in ML Ops to measure and track model performance, experience working with MLFlow.
Experience with Spark or other distributed computing frameworks.
Experience in ML platforms like Sage maker, Kubeflow.
Experience with pipeline orchestration tools such Airflow.
Experience in deploying models to cloud services like AWS, Azure, GCP, Azure ML.
Expertise in SQL, SQL DB's.
Knowledgeable of core CS concepts such as common data structures and algorithms.
Collaborate well with teams with different backgrounds / expertise / functions.
Fluency in English.
Client-first mentality.
Intense work ethic.
Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach.
